description: Miles Davis at Fillmore is a 1970 live album by the jazz trumpeter Miles Davis and band, recorded at the Fillmore East, New York City on four consecutive days, June 17 through June 20, 1970, originally released as a double vinyl LP. The performances featured the double keyboard set-up Davis toured with for a few months, with Keith Jarrett and Chick Corea playing electronic organ and Fender Rhodes electric piano, respectively. The group opened for Laura Nyro at these performances.
significance: Documented Miles's successful crossover to rock audiences. The band's energy and volume influenced both jazz and rock musicians.

title: Ptah, the El Daoud
label: Impulse!
description: Ptah, the El Daoud is the third solo album by American jazz pianist and harpist Alice Coltrane, released on Impulse! Records in September 1970. The album was recorded in the basement of her home in Dix Hills, New York, in a session on January 26, 1970.
significance: Showed Alice developing her own compositional voice. The combination of two contrasting tenor saxophonists creates a unique sound.

title: Red Clay
label: CTI
description: Red Clay is an album recorded in 1970 by jazz trumpeter Freddie Hubbard. It was his first album on Creed Taylor's CTI label and marked a shift toward the soul-jazz fusion sounds that would dominate his recordings in the later part of the decade. It entered at number 20 on Billboard’s Top 20 Best Selling Jazz LPs, on June 20, 1970.
significance: Bridged hard bop and fusion without losing artistic credibility. CTI's lush production was influential.

title: Superimposition
label: Tico
description: Superimposition is the twelfth studio album by American pianist Eddie Palmieri. It was released by Tico Records in 1970. The album combines modern salsa renditions of Cuban standards on side A with experimental descargas on side B. Since its release, the album has been praised by critics such as John Storm Roberts for its innovative approach.
significance: An important entry in Eddie Palmieri's discography, showcasing their contribution to latin jazz.
keyTracks: Que lindo eso, eh! / Isn't It Pretty

title: The Diverse Yusef Lateef
label: Atlantic
description: The Diverse Yusef Lateef is a jazz album by saxophonist Yusef Lateef released in 1970. In it are mixed influences from rhythm and blues and soul music and world music.
significance: Demonstrates Yusef Lateef's command of jazz and their place within the broader jazz lineage.

title: The Leon Thomas Album
label: Flying Dutchman
description: The Leon Thomas Album is the second album by American jazz vocalist and percussionist Leon Thomas recorded in 1970 and released by the Flying Dutchman label.
significance: An important entry in Leon Thomas's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Transition
label: impulse!
description: Transition is an album of music by jazz saxophonist John Coltrane, recorded in 1965 but released posthumously only in 1970. As its title indicates, Transition was a bridge between classic quartet recordings like A Love Supreme and the more experimental works of Coltrane's last years.
significance: An essential document of John Coltrane's artistry during the free jazz period.
keyTracks: Suite: Prayer and Meditation: Day / Peace and After / Prayer and Meditation: Evening / Affirmation / Prayer and Meditation: 4 A.M.

title: Escalator over the Hill
label: JCOA
description: Escalator over the Hill is mostly referred to as a jazz opera, but it was released as a "chronotransduction", with "words by Paul Haines, adaptation and music by Carla Bley, production and coordination by Michael Mantler", performed by the Jazz Composer's Orchestra.
significance: The most ambitious jazz composition of its era. Bley's vision created a work that remains unmatched in scope and eclecticism.

title: Harlem Bush Music: Taifa
label: Milestone
description: Bartz's NTU Troop delivers one of the most powerful spiritual jazz statements of the early 1970s. The album channels African rhythms, free improvisation, and Black consciousness into a urgent, uncompromising whole that pulses with communal energy.
significance: A landmark of politically engaged spiritual jazz that captured the cultural ferment of early-70s Harlem. Bartz's alto saxophone work here ranks among the most passionate of the era.

title: Harlem River Drive
label: Roulette
description: Puerto Rican piano legend Eddie Palmieri's genre-shattering album fuses salsa, soul, funk, and jazz into a revolutionary Nuyorican sound. The extended jams build hypnotic grooves that blend clave rhythms with wah-wah guitar and horn arrangements of breathtaking power.
significance: A milestone in Latin music that broke down the walls between salsa and jazz-funk. Palmieri's visionary fusion of Afro-Caribbean and African-American musical traditions anticipated decades of cross-cultural experimentation.

title: Journey in Satchidananda
label: Impulse!
description: Journey in Satchidananda is the fourth studio album by American jazz pianist and harpist Alice Coltrane, released in February 1971 on Impulse! Records. The first four tracks were recorded at Coltrane's home studio in Dix Hills, New York, in November 1970, while "Isis and Osiris" was recorded live at the Village Gate in Greenwich Village in July of that year. Coltrane is joined on the album by saxophonist Pharoah Sanders, bassists Cecil McBee and Charlie Haden, and drummer Rashied Ali.
significance: Established Alice Coltrane as a major artist in her own right, not just John's widow. Her synthesis of Indian classical music, jazz, and spiritual devotion created a new genre.

title: Lift Every Voice and Sing
label: Atlantic
description: Lift Every Voice and Sing is an album by American jazz drummer Max Roach with the J.C. White Singers recorded in 1971 and released on the Atlantic label.
significance: An essential document of Max Roach's artistry during the jazz fusion period.
keyTracks: Were You There When They Crucified My Lord

title: Mwandishi
label: Warner Bros. Records
description: Mwandishi is the ninth studio album by American jazz pianist Herbie Hancock, released in March 1971. It is the first album to officially feature Hancock’s ‘Mwandishi’ sextet, consisting of reed player Bennie Maupin, trumpeter Eddie Henderson, trombonist Julian Priester, bassist Buster Williams, and drummer Billy Hart.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Herbie Hancock's evolving artistic vision.
keyTracks: You'll Know When You Get There

title: On With It!
label: Prestige
description: On With It! is a 1971 jazz album by Brother Jack McDuff, released on the Prestige label. The album blends elements of jazz, soul jazz. Notable tracks include "Hey Lawdy Mama", "The Last Goodun", "Dink's Dream", "Drown in My Own Tears".
significance: An important entry in Brother Jack McDuff's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Solo Guitar
label: Incus
description: Derek Bailey's pioneering solo guitar recording strips the instrument of all conventional technique and rebuilds it from the ground up. His 'non-idiomatic improvisation' treats the guitar as a pure sound source, producing music of startling originality and uncompromising abstraction.
significance: One of the founding documents of free improvisation. Bailey's radical approach to the guitar influenced generations of experimental musicians across jazz, rock, and electronic music.

title: Spirits
label: Birth Records
description: Spirits is a 1971 free jazz album by Jeanne Lee, released on the Birth Records label. The album blends elements of free jazz, avant-garde jazz. Notable tracks include "No. 90 26871-02 NY", "No. 88 26871-02 NY", "Continuing From Side 1, No 88", "No. 92 26871-02 NY".
significance: An important entry in Jeanne Lee's discography, showcasing their contribution to free jazz.

title: The Odyssey of Iska
label: Blue Note
description: The Odyssey of Iska is a 1971 jazz fusion album by Wayne Shorter, released on the Blue Note label. The album blends elements of jazz fusion, avant-garde jazz. Notable tracks include "Wind", "Storm", "Calm", "De Pois Do Amor, O Vazio".
significance: Showed Shorter's restless creativity as he moved from acoustic to electric jazz. It presaged his Weather Report innovations.
keyTracks: De Pois Do Amor, O Vazio

title: Thembi
label: Impulse!
description: Named after Sanders' wife, Thembi finds the saxophonist in a more lyrical, accessible mood than his earlier fire music. The album weaves African percussion, flute, and vocal chants into a warm, meditative tapestry that anticipates world music fusion.
significance: Showed the gentler, melodic side of Sanders' art without sacrificing spiritual intensity. The album's Afrocentric grooves influenced generations of jazz and electronic musicians.

title: Universal Consciousness
label: Impulse!
description: Universal Consciousness is the fifth studio album by American jazz musician Alice Coltrane, released in September 1971 on Impulse! Records. The album was recorded from April to June 1971 at A & R Recording in New York City and the Coltrane home studios in Dix Hills, New York.
significance: One of the most unusual jazz albums ever made. The combination of free jazz and orchestral strings influenced later spiritual jazz composers.

title: Weather Report
label: Columbia
description: Weather Report is the 1971 debut album by the jazz fusion group Weather Report, released on Columbia. Formed by keyboardist Joe Zawinul and saxophonist Wayne Shorter after both had worked with Miles Davis, the group pioneered a new direction in jazz fusion. The album features tracks such as "Milky Way", "Umbrellas", "Seventh Arrow", and "Orange Lady".
significance: A 1971 avant-garde jazz release by Weather Report, representing their creative work during this period.

title: African Cookbook
label: Atlantic
description: African Cookbook is a 1972 jazz album by Randy Weston, released on the Atlantic label. The album blends elements of jazz, post-bop. Notable tracks include "Berkshire Blues", "Portrait of Vivian", "Willie's Tune", "Niger Mambo".
significance: An important entry in Randy Weston's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Art Blakey & His Jazz Messengers
label: impulse!
description: A showcase for Art Blakey's drums, Art Blakey & His Jazz Messengers (1972) draws from the hard bop vocabulary with authority. Tracks such as 'Alamode' and 'Invitation' anchor the program.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Art Blakey's evolving artistic vision.
keyTracks: You Don't Know What Love Is

title: Black Is the Color
label: Milestone
description: Black Is the Color is an album by the American saxophonist Joe Henderson, released in 1972 on Milestone. The original idea for the album was "to approach it entirely from the standpoint of having no pre-conceived ideas for the musicians to relate to." However, after listening to a tape copy of one segment of the original session, the saxophonist, "became aware of further possibilities.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Joe Henderson's evolving artistic vision.
keyTracks: Black Is the Color (Of My True Love's Mind)

title: Breakthrough!
label: Unknown
description: Breakthrough! is an album by the Cedar Walton/Hank Mobley Quintet recorded on February 22, 1972, originally released on the short-lived Cobblestone label and later reissued on Muse. It features performances by Mobley and Walton with baritone saxophonist Charles Davis, bassist Sam Jones, and Mobley’s longtime drummer Billy Higgins. This was Mobley's final studio recording before retiring due to health issues.
significance: An important entry in Cedar Walton's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Connection
label: Columbia
description: Connection is an album by trumpeter/bandleader Don Ellis recorded in 1972 and released on the Columbia label. The album features big band arrangements of pop hits of the day along with Ellis' "Theme from The French Connection", which won him a Grammy Award for Best Instrumental Arrangement in 1973.
significance: An important entry in Don Ellis's discography, showcasing their contribution to big band.
keyTracks: Put It Where You Want It

title: Dig This!
label: Blue Note
description: Dig This! is a 1972 jazz fusion album by Bobbi Humphrey, released on the Blue Note label. Notable tracks include "Lonely Town, Lonely Street", "Is This All", "Smiling Faces Sometimes", "Virtue".
significance: An important entry in Bobbi Humphrey's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz fusion.
keyTracks: I Love Every Little Thing About You

title: Dogon A.D.
label: Mbari
description: St. Louis saxophonist Julius Hemphill's landmark recording draws on the cosmology of West Africa's Dogon people to create music of extraordinary originality. With cellist Abdul Wadud and drummer Phillip Wilson, Hemphill crafts extended pieces that combine blues, free jazz, and African-influenced rhythms.
significance: One of the greatest independent jazz recordings of the 1970s. Hemphill's compositions—particularly the extended title track—demonstrated a new approach to structure in free jazz that influenced the entire loft scene.

title: Ella Loves Cole
label: Atlantic
description: Ella Loves Cole is a 1972 jazz album by Ella Fitzgerald, released on the Atlantic label. The album blends elements of jazz, vocal jazz. Notable tracks include "I Get a Kick Out of You", "Down in the Depths", "At Long Last Love", "I’ve Got You Under My Skin".
significance: An important entry in Ella Fitzgerald's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: I Get a Kick Out of You
I’ve Got You Under My Skin
So Near and Yet So Far

title: Facing You
label: ECM
description: Facing You is a solo album by jazz pianist Keith Jarrett recorded on November 10, 1971, and released on ECM March the following year as Jarrett's first with the label. The album is praised as "a hallmark recording of solo piano" that "altered the course of jazz".
significance: Launched Jarrett's solo career and helped define the ECM aesthetic. The album's success proved audiences would embrace pure improvisation.

title: I Sing the Body Electric
label: Columbia
description: I Sing the Body Electric is the second studio album released by the American jazz fusion band Weather Report in 1972.
significance: Demonstrates Weather Report's command of avant-garde jazz and their place within the broader jazz lineage.
keyTracks: Medley: Vertical Invader / T.H. / Dr Honoris Causa

title: Let My Children Hear Music
label: Columbia
description: Let My Children Hear Music is an album released by Columbia Records in 1972 of music by composer Charles Mingus, produced by Teo Macero. The music is scored for large jazz orchestra and Mingus worked with several arrangers, orchestrators and conductors, most notably Sy Johnson and Alan Raph, to realize some of his most ambitious compositions. In the original liner notes, Mingus described it as "the best album I have ever made".
significance: Demonstrates Charles Mingus's command of cool jazz and their place within the broader jazz lineage.
keyTracks: The Shoes of the Fisherman’s Wife Are Some Jive Ass Slippers
Don’t Be Afraid, the Clown’s Afraid Too

title: Mystic Brew
label: Blue Note
description: Mystic Brew is a 1972 soul jazz album by Ronnie Foster, released on the Blue Note label. The album blends elements of soul jazz, jazz-funk. Notable tracks include "Mystic Brew", "Summer Song", "Chunky".
significance: Bridged soul jazz and the emerging funk movement. Its influence on hip-hop cannot be overstated.

title: On the Corner
label: Columbia
description: On the Corner is a studio album by the American jazz trumpeter, bandleader, and composer Miles Davis. It was recorded in June and July 1972 and released on October 11 of that year by Columbia Records. The album continued Davis' exploration of jazz fusion, and explicitly drew on the funk of Sly Stone and James Brown, the experimental music of Karlheinz Stockhausen, the free jazz of Ornette Coleman, and the work of collaborator Paul Buckmaster.
significance: Ahead of its time, it influenced hip-hop, electronic music, and post-punk. Its reputation has grown enormously.

title: Return to Forever
label: ECM
description: Return to Forever is a jazz fusion album by Chick Corea recorded over two days in February 1972 but was not released in the USA until 1975—Corea's fourth release for the label. It is the debut of a quintet featuring singer Flora Purim, flautist/saxophonist Joe Farrell, bassist Stanley Clarke and percussionist Airto Moreira, who would go on to record under the name Return to Forever.
significance: Helped define the ECM sound and the more lyrical side of fusion. The band became one of the most influential fusion groups.
keyTracks: What Game Shall We Play Today

title: Sahara
label: Milestone
description: Sahara is the twelfth album by jazz pianist McCoy Tyner, his first to be released on the Milestone label. It was recorded in January 1972 and features performances by Tyner with saxophonist Sonny Fortune, bassist Calvin Hill, and drummer Alphonse Mouzon. The music shows African and Eastern influences and features all the musicians playing multiple instruments, with Tyner himself utilizing koto, flute, and percussion in addition to his usual piano.
significance: Demonstrated that spiritual jazz could be powerful rather than merely meditative. Tyner's percussive attack influenced generations of pianists.

title: Science Fiction
label: Columbia
description: Science Fiction is an album by the American avant-garde jazz saxophonist and composer Ornette Coleman, recorded in September and October 1971 and released on Columbia Records in February 1972.
significance: A noteworthy album in Ornette Coleman's body of work within the jazz fusion tradition.

title: Shades of Green
label: Blue Note
description: Shades of Green is an album by American jazz guitarist Grant Green featuring performances recorded in 1971 and released on the Blue Note label.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Grant Green's evolving artistic vision.
keyTracks: I Don't Want Nobody to Give Me Nothing (Open Up the Door I'll Get It Myself) / Cold Sweat (medley)

title: Skies of America
label: Columbia
description: Skies of America is the 17th album by jazz saxophonist Ornette Coleman, released on Columbia Records in 1972. It consists of one long composition by Coleman taking up both sides of the album, played by the London Symphony Orchestra and conducted by David Measham. Coleman himself only plays on a few segments, and there is no other jazz instrumentation.
significance: An important entry in Ornette Coleman's discography, showcasing their contribution to avant-garde jazz.

title: Spiritual Nature
label: Three Blind Mice
description: Spiritual Nature is a 1972 spiritual jazz album by Terumasa Hino, released on the Three Blind Mice label. The album blends elements of spiritual jazz, Japanese jazz. Notable tracks include "Mellow Dream", "Alone, Alone and Alone", "Spiritual Nature".
significance: Showed that spiritual jazz could transcend cultural boundaries. Hino became Japan's most internationally recognized jazz artist.

title: Taking Some Time
label: Polydor
description: Taking Some Time is a 1972 jazz-funk album by Roy Ayers, released on the Polydor label. The album blends elements of jazz-funk, soul jazz. Notable tracks include "We Live in Brooklyn, Baby", "Ain't Got Time", "Pretty Brown Skin".
significance: A cornerstone of jazz-funk that became essential hip-hop source material. 'Everybody Loves the Sunshine' from this era is iconic.

title: The Great American Songbook Live
label: Atlantic
description: The Great American Songbook Live is a 1972 vocal jazz album by Carmen McRae, released on the Atlantic label. Notable tracks include "Alfie", "If You Could See Me Now", "Baltimore Oriole", "I'm Always Drunk in San Francisco".
significance: Captured McRae's complete artistry—singer, pianist, and entertainer. Her spoken introductions became legendary.
keyTracks: If You Could See Me Now
I'm Always Drunk in San Francisco

title: The Visit!
label: Cobblestone
description: The Visit! is a 1972 jazz album by Pat Martino, released on the Cobblestone label. The album blends elements of jazz, post-bop. Notable tracks include "The Visit", "What Are You Doing the Rest of Your Life?", "Road Song", "Footprints".
significance: An important entry in Pat Martino's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: What Are You Doing the Rest of Your Life?

title: Vietnam
label: ESP-Disk
description: Vietnam, also referred to as Vietnam 1 & 2  is a live album by the Revolutionary Ensemble, violinist Leroy Jenkins, bassist Sirone and drummer Jerome Cooper, which was recorded in 1972 and released on the ESP-Disk label.
significance: An important entry in Revolutionary Ensemble's discography, showcasing their contribution to free jazz.

title: World Galaxy
label: Impulse!
description: Alice Coltrane's most orchestrally ambitious album, featuring her harp and organ work drenched in lush string arrangements. The music travels through cosmic landscapes, from the devotional opening of 'My Favorite Things' to the transcendent closer 'A Galaxy of Love.'
significance: Represented Coltrane's boldest fusion of jazz, Indian classical music, and orchestral grandeur. The album's cosmic spirituality has been rediscovered and sampled extensively in hip-hop and electronic music.

title: Birds of Fire
label: Columbia
description: Birds of Fire is the second studio album by jazz fusion band the Mahavishnu Orchestra. It was released in 1973 by Columbia Records and is the last studio album released by the original line-up before it dissolved.
significance: Defined the louder, more aggressive side of fusion. Its influence on progressive rock was immense.

title: Black Byrd
label: Blue Note
description: Trumpeter Donald Byrd's commercial breakthrough, produced by the Mizell Brothers, was Blue Note's biggest-selling album ever at the time. The music wraps Byrd's warm trumpet tone in layers of funky clavinet, wah-wah guitar, and string arrangements that still sound fresh today.
significance: Saved Blue Note Records from bankruptcy and proved that jazz could sell without compromising artistic integrity. The album's smooth jazz-funk template influenced a generation of fusion artists.

title: Blacks and Blues
label: Blue Note
description: Flutist Bobbi Humphrey's landmark album, produced by the Mizell Brothers, merges jazz flute with irresistible funk grooves and lush string arrangements. The title track and 'Harlem River Drive' pulse with a sophisticated groove that sits perfectly between jazz and R&B.
significance: One of the most successful Blue Note albums of the 1970s and a milestone for women in jazz. Humphrey's accessible, funky approach brought jazz flute to pop audiences and influenced countless producers.

title: Capra Black
label: Strata-East
description: Capra Black is a 1973 spiritual jazz album by Billy Harper, released on the Strata-East label. The album blends elements of spiritual jazz, post-bop. Notable tracks include "Capra Black", "Cry of Hunger", "Soulful Song for My Grandfather".
significance: A Strata-East label classic that documents the post-Coltrane spiritual jazz scene at its peak. Harper's influence on David Murray and other next-generation players was profound.

title: Conference of the Birds
label: ECM
description: Conference of the Birds is an album by the Dave Holland Quartet, recorded on 30 November 1972 and released on ECM the following year—Holland's debut as bandleader and fourth project for the label. The quartet features alto saxophonist Anthony Braxton, tenor saxophonist Sam Rivers, and percussionist Barry Altschul.
significance: Defined a new approach to small-group jazz—collective, compositionally sophisticated, and dynamically subtle. A template for 1970s avant-garde.

title: Head Hunters
label: Columbia
description: A groundbreaking fusion of jazz, funk, and electronic music that redefined what jazz could sound like. Recorded in 1973 with the Headhunters band—Bennie Maupin on saxophones, Paul Jackson on bass, Harvey Mason on drums, and Bill Summers on percussion—Hancock layered Fender Rhodes, clavinet, and ARP synthesizers over deep, hypnotic grooves. The 15-minute 'Chameleon' opens with one of the most iconic bass lines in music history. 'Watermelon Man' reimagines Hancock's earlier hit with African-influenced percussion.
significance: Became the first jazz album to go platinum, proving jazz could reach a mass audience without sacrificing artistic integrity. Its funky, synthesizer-driven sound directly influenced the birth of hip-hop, acid jazz, and electronic dance music. Countless artists have sampled these tracks, from Us3 to A Tribe Called Quest.

title: In the Townships
label: Caroline
description: South African alto saxophonist Dudu Pukwana's exhilarating fusion of township jive with British free jazz, recorded in London exile. The music captures the irrepressible joy and pain of the South African diaspora, combining marabi rhythms with avant-garde intensity.
significance: One of the finest albums to emerge from the South African exile community in London. Pukwana's ability to bridge township music and free improvisation created a uniquely powerful hybrid.

title: Izipho Zam (My Gifts)
label: Strata-East
description: Izipho Zam (My Gifts) is the third album led by American saxophonist Pharoah Sanders. The album was recorded in 1969 but not released on the Strata-East label until 1973. It features Sanders with a large ensemble.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Pharoah Sanders's evolving artistic vision.

title: Lawrence of Newark
label: Perception Records
description: Lawrence of Newark is a 1973 jazz album by Larry Young, released on the Perception Records label. Notable tracks include "Saudia", "Alive", "Hello Your Quietness (Islands)", "Sunshine Fly Away".
significance: An important entry in Larry Young's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: Khalid of Space, Part Two: Welcome

title: Love Devotion Surrender
label: Columbia
description: Love Devotion Surrender is an album released in 1973 by guitarists Carlos Santana and John McLaughlin, with the backing of their respective bands, Santana and The Mahavishnu Orchestra. The album was inspired by the teachings of Sri Chinmoy and intended as a tribute to John Coltrane. It contains two Coltrane compositions, two McLaughlin songs, and a traditional gospel song arranged by Santana and McLaughlin. It was certified Gold in 1973.
significance: An important entry in John McLaughlin's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz fusion.
keyTracks: Let Us Go Into the House of the Lord

title: Previously Unreleased Recordings
label: Verve
description: Previously Unreleased Recordings is an album by saxophonist Sonny Stitt featuring compositions associated with Duke Ellington which was originally recorded in 1960 and released on the Verve label in 1973. On CD it can be found on Import as part of Rearin' Back / Tribute To Duke Ellington
significance: An important entry in Ben Webster's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: I'll Always Be in Love With You

title: Solo Concerts: Bremen / Lausanne
label: ECM
description: Solo Concerts: Bremen / Lausanne is a 1973 solo piano album by Keith Jarrett, released on the ECM label. The album blends elements of solo piano, improvised music. Notable tracks include "Bremen, July 12, 1973", "Lausanne, March 20, 1973".
significance: Paved the way for The Köln Concert and proved that totally improvised solo piano could captivate audiences.

title: Soul Makossa
label: Fiesta
description: Emmanuel N'Djoké "Manu" Dibango was a Cameroonian musician and songwriter who played saxophone and vibraphone. He developed a musical style fusing jazz, funk, and traditional Cameroonian music. His father was a member of the Yabassi ethnic group, while his mother was a Duala. He was best known for his 1972 single "Soul Makossa". The song has been referred to as the most sampled African song in addition Dibango, himself, as the most sampled African musician in history. He died from COVID-19 on 24 March 2020.
significance: Pioneered Afrobeat before Fela. Michael Jackson's 'Wanna Be Startin' Somethin'' borrowed its chant, creating one of music's first copyright disputes.

title: Space Is the Place
label: Blue Thumb
description: The soundtrack to Sun Ra's only feature film, blending cosmic philosophy with avant-garde jazz. Ra's Arkestra creates otherworldly textures using electronic keyboards, African percussion, and free improvisation.
significance: The definitive statement of Sun Ra's Afrofuturist vision. The album posits space as a metaphor for Black liberation and imagination, influencing generations of musicians from George Clinton to Flying Lotus.

title: Spectrum
label: Atlantic
description: Spectrum is a 1973 jazz rock album by Billy Cobham, released on the Atlantic label. The album blends elements of jazz rock, jazz fusion. Notable tracks include "Quadrant 4", "Stratus", "Red Baron", "Spectrum".
significance: One of fusion's most influential albums. Its virtuosity and power influenced rock and metal drummers.

title: The Beginning and the End
label: Columbia
description: The Beginning and the End is a Clifford Brown compilation album. The album opens with two tracks that Clifford Brown recorded with Chris Powell's Blue Flames in 1952, and ends with recordings of a session held at Music City Club in Philadelphia in 1956. According to the liner notes, they are "The first and last recorded performances of one of the greatest soloists in the history of Jazz." According to Nick Catalano's biography of Clifford Brown, the Music City Club session could have taken place on May 31, 1955.
significance: An important entry in Clifford Brown's discography, showcasing their contribution to hard bop.

title: Belonging
label: ECM
description: Belonging is a 1974 contemporary jazz album by Keith Jarrett European Quartet, released on the ECM label. The album blends elements of contemporary jazz, chamber jazz. Notable tracks include "Spiral Dance", "Long as You Know You're Living Yours", "Belonging", "The Windup".
significance: Created a distinctly European jazz sound. The quartet's interplay influenced countless European musicians.
keyTracks: Long as You Know You're Living Yours

title: Experience and Judgment
label: Atlantic
description: Vocalist and pianist Andy Bey's deeply personal album blends jazz, soul, and spiritual themes with his extraordinary baritone voice—one of the richest and most flexible in jazz. Horace Silver provides the arrangements, and the music radiates with warm, devotional energy.
significance: A lost classic of spiritual vocal jazz that has been rediscovered and championed by a new generation. Bey's four-octave voice and uncompromising artistry make this one of the most powerful vocal jazz recordings of the 1970s.

title: Illuminations
label: Columbia
description: On Illuminations (1974), Alice Coltrane delivers a focused exploration of free jazz, recorded for Columbia. Among its key performances are 'Guru Sri Chinmoy Aphorism' and 'Angel of Air / Angel of Water'.
significance: Adds to Alice Coltrane's reputation as a leading voice in free jazz.
keyTracks: Angel of Air / Angel of Water

title: Love, Sex, and the Zodiac
label: Fantasy
description: Love, Sex, and the Zodiac is an album by jazz saxophonist Cannonball Adderley recorded in Berkeley, California in 1970, but not released on the Fantasy label until 1974, featuring performances by Adderley's Quintet featuring Nat Adderley, Hal Galper, Walter Booker and Roy McCurdy with guest appearances by George Duke and Jimmy Jones and narration by Los Angeles DJ Rick Holmes.
significance: Adds to Cannonball Adderley's reputation as a leading voice in jazz.

title: Mannenberg - Is Where It's Happening
label: Sun
description: Mannenberg - Is Where It's Happening is a 1974 Cape jazz album by Abdullah Ibrahim, released on the Sun label. The album blends elements of Cape jazz, South African jazz. Notable tracks include "Mannenberg", "Soweto Is Where It's At", "Cape Town Fringe", "The Pilgrim".
significance: The most important South African jazz recording. 'Mannenberg' became a rallying cry for freedom, played at protests and celebrations.

title: Mannenberg ‘Is Where It’s Happening’
label: The Sun
description: Mannenberg ‘Is Where It’s Happening’ is a 1974 jazz album by Abdullah Ibrahim, released on the The Sun label. Notable tracks include "Mannenberg", "The Pilgrim".
significance: An important entry in Abdullah Ibrahim's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Mbali Africa
label: Flying Disk
description: Mbali Africa is a 1974 afro jazz album by Sadao Watanabe, released on the Flying Disk label. The album blends elements of afro jazz, jazz fusion. Notable tracks include "Mbali Africa", "Kenya Ya", "Masai", "Jambo".
significance: A pioneering cross-cultural collaboration. Watanabe helped establish jazz as a global music, not just American or European.

title: On the Avenue
label: Blue Note
description: On the Avenue is a 1974 jazz-funk album by Ronnie Foster, released on the Blue Note label. Notable tracks include "Serenade to a Rock", "On the Avenue", "What Happened to the Sunshine", "Golden Lady".
significance: An important entry in Ronnie Foster's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz-funk.

title: Satin Doll
label: Blue Note
description: Satin Doll is a 1974 jazz album by Bobbi Humphrey, released on the Blue Note label. The album blends elements of jazz, jazz-funk, soul jazz. Notable tracks include "New York Times", "Satin Doll", "San Francisco Lights", "Ladies Day".
significance: An important entry in Bobbi Humphrey's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Summit (Reunión cumbre)
label: Erre T.V.
description: Gerry Mulligan's Summit (Reunión cumbre), cut for Erre T.V. in 1974, stands as a distinguished entry in the cool jazz canon. The album channels the electric energy and cross-genre experimentation of the fusion era. Notable tracks include '20 Years Ago' and 'Close Your Eyes and Listen'.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Gerry Mulligan's evolving artistic vision.

title: Thrust
label: Columbia
description: Thrust is the fourteenth studio album by American jazz-funk musician Herbie Hancock, released in September 1974 on Columbia Records. The album reached No. 2 on the Billboard Top Soul Albums chart and No. 13 on the Billboard 200 chart. It is the second album featuring the Headhunters: saxophonist Bennie Maupin, bass guitarist Paul Jackson, drummer Mike Clark and percussionist Bill Summers.
significance: Solidified Hancock's position as fusion's most commercially successful artist while maintaining artistic integrity.

title: Witchi-Tai-To
label: ECM
description: Witchi-Tai-To is an album by the Jan Garbarek–Bobo Stenson Quartet recorded over two days in November 1973 and released on ECM the following year. The quartet features rhythm section Palle Danielsson and Jon Christensen.
significance: Established Garbarek as ECM's signature artist. The album's mix of world music, free jazz, and Nordic atmospherics defined a new style.

title: Agharta
label: CBS
description: Agharta is a live double album by American jazz trumpeter, composer, and bandleader Miles Davis. It was first released in Japan by CBS/Sony in August 1975. By the time he recorded the album, Davis was 48 years old and had alienated many in the jazz community while attracting younger rock audiences with his radical electric fusion music. After experimenting with different line-ups, he established a stable live band in 1973 and toured constantly for the next two years, despite physical pain from worsening health and emotional instability brought on by substance abuse.
significance: A noteworthy album in Miles Davis's body of work within the jazz fusion tradition.

title: Air Song
label: Whynot
description: Henry Threadgill's trio Air—with bassist Fred Hopkins and drummer Steve McCall—delivers one of the loft jazz era's most inventive recordings. The group's collective interplay is extraordinary, with each member contributing equally to the music's shifting textures and dynamic contrasts.
significance: Air represented a new model for the jazz trio, one based on collective improvisation rather than soloist-plus-rhythm-section. Threadgill's unconventional compositions and multi-instrumental approach pointed toward jazz's future.

title: Concierto
label: CTI
description: Jim Hall's masterwork reimagines Joaquin Rodrigo's 'Concierto de Aranjuez' for jazz guitar and orchestra, with arrangements by Don Sebesky. Hall's warm, introspective guitar tone weaves through the orchestral textures with the elegance and emotional depth of a classical soloist.
significance: One of the finest CTI recordings and a landmark of jazz guitar. Hall's restrained, lyrical approach proved that jazz guitar could achieve the depth and subtlety of the finest chamber music.
keyTracks: You'd Be So Nice to Come Home To

title: Eastern Rebellion
label: Timeless
description: Pianist Cedar Walton leads a powerhouse quartet with Bob Berg, Sam Jones, and Billy Higgins through a program of his finest compositions. Walton's sophisticated harmonic sense and effortless swing make this one of the greatest straight-ahead jazz piano albums of the 1970s.
significance: Proved that hard bop was alive and well during the fusion era. Walton's compositional brilliance—heard in classics like 'Naima's Love Song'—established him as one of jazz's most important composer-pianists.

title: Expansions
label: Flying Dutchman
description: Keyboardist Lonnie Liston Smith and the Cosmic Echoes deliver an album of shimmering, celestial jazz-funk that bridges the cosmic spirituality of Pharoah Sanders with the dancefloor accessibility of Earth, Wind & Fire. The title track's floating Rhodes piano and ethereal vocals are sublime.
significance: A cornerstone of the rare groove and spiritual jazz movements. The album's cosmic, ethereal sound has been sampled extensively in hip-hop and electronic music, making Smith a cult figure across multiple genres.

title: Funky Stuff
label: Philips
description: Japanese saxophonist Jiro Inagaki and his Soul Media deliver irresistible jazz-funk grooves deeply indebted to CTI-era fusion and Blaxploitation soundtracks. Tight horn arrangements, wah-wah guitar, and infectious rhythms make this a dancefloor essential.
significance: A prized find in the rare groove and Japanese jazz collector communities. The album exemplifies the distinctive Japanese approach to jazz-funk that has influenced contemporary producers worldwide.

title: Larry Young's Fuel
label: Arista
description: Larry Young's Fuel is a 1975 jazz album by Larry Young, released on the Arista label. The album blends elements of jazz, jazz-funk. Notable tracks include "Fuel for the Fire", "I Ching (Book of Changes)", "Turn Off the Lights", "Floating".
significance: An important entry in Larry Young's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Lone-Lee
label: SteepleChase
description: Lone-Lee is a solo album by American jazz saxophonist Lee Konitz, recorded in Denmark in 1974 and released on the Danish SteepleChase label. The original LP, released in 1975, featured an edited take of "The Song Is You" lasting 19 minutes, while the 1987 CD release featured the complete unedited take.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Lee Konitz's evolving artistic vision.

title: Old / Quartet
label: Nessa Records
description: Old / Quartet is a 1975 jazz album by Roscoe Mitchell, released on the Nessa Records label. Notable tracks include "Old", "Quartet, Part 1", "Quartet, Part 2", "Solo".
significance: An important entry in Roscoe Mitchell's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Places and Spaces
label: Blue Note
description: Places and Spaces is a 1975 jazz-funk album by Donald Byrd, released on the Blue Note label. The album blends elements of jazz-funk, jazz fusion. Notable tracks include "Change (Makes You Want to Hustle)", "Places and Spaces", "Wind Parade".
significance: Bridged jazz and R&B audiences. Its influence on hip-hop production has been immense.

title: Saxophone Solos
label: Incus
description: British saxophonist Evan Parker's landmark solo recording pushes the soprano and tenor saxophones into entirely new sonic territory. Using circular breathing, multiphonics, and overblowing, Parker creates dense, spiraling sound-worlds that seem to defy the physical limitations of the instrument.
significance: Redefined what was possible on the saxophone. Parker's extended techniques and approach to solo performance influenced virtually every experimental saxophonist who followed.

title: Silent Tongues
label: Freedom
description: Silent Tongues is a live album by Cecil Taylor on solo piano recorded at the Montreux Jazz Festival in 1974. It features Taylor's five-movement work "Silent Tongues", along with two encores.
significance: The definitive solo Taylor document. His approach to the piano as percussion instrument reaches full maturity.

title: Silver 'n Brass
label: Blue Note
description: Silver 'n Brass is an album by jazz pianist Horace Silver released on the Blue Note label in 1975, featuring performances by Silver with Tom Harrell, Bob Berg, Ron Carter, Al Foster, Bob Cranshaw, and Bernard Purdie with an overdubbed brass section arranged by Wade Marcus featuring Oscar Brashear, Bobby Bryant, Vincent DeRosa, Frank Rosolino, Maurice Spears, Jerome Richardson, and Buddy Collette.
significance: An important entry in Horace Silver's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Taking Off
label: Warner Bros. Records
description: Taking Off is a 1975 jazz album by David Sanborn, released on the Warner Bros. Records label. The album blends elements of jazz, jazz-funk, smooth jazz. Notable tracks include "Butterfat", "'way 'Cross Georgia", "Duck Ankles", "Funky Banana".
significance: An important entry in David Sanborn's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Tale Spinnin’
label: Columbia
description: Tale Spinnin’ is a 1975 jazz fusion album by Weather Report, released on the Columbia label. The album blends elements of jazz fusion, jazz. Notable tracks include "Man in the Green Shirt", "Lusitanos", "Between the Thighs", "Badia".
significance: An important entry in Weather Report's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz fusion.

title: The Köln Concert
label: ECM
description: The Köln Concert is a live solo double album by pianist Keith Jarrett recorded at the Opera House in Köln, West Germany, on 24 January 1975 and released on ECM Records later that year. It is the best-selling solo album in jazz history and the best-selling piano album. In 2025, the album was deemed "culturally, historically, or aesthetically significant" by the Library of Congress and selected for preservation in the National Recording Registry.
significance: Proved that totally improvised music could reach a mass audience. Its influence on solo piano music has been immense.

title: The Psyche
label: RE: Records
description: The Psyche is a live album by the Revolutionary Ensemble, violinist Leroy Jenkins, bassist Sirone and drummer Jerome Cooper. It was initially released on LP in 1975 by the group's own label RE Records, and was reissued on CD in 2002 by Mutable Music.
significance: An important entry in Revolutionary Ensemble's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: The Real Book
label: Bootleg
description: The Real Book is a 1975 jazz standards album by Various Artists, released on the Bootleg label. Notable tracks include "All The Things You Are", "Autumn Leaves", "Blue Bossa".
significance: Became the de facto curriculum for jazz education. Its illegal origins and eventual legitimization reflect jazz's complex relationship with the music industry.

title: A Ghetto Lullaby
label: Inner City Records
description: A Ghetto Lullaby is a live album by American saxophonist Jackie McLean recorded at the Jazzhus Montmartre in 1973 and released on the SteepleChase label.
significance: An essential document of Jackie McLean's artistry during the jazz fusion period.

title: Balladyna
label: ECM
description: Balladyna is an album by Polish jazz trumpeter and composer Tomasz Stańko recorded in December 1975 and released on ECM in April the following year. The quartet features saxophonist Tomasz Szukalski and rhythm section Dave Holland and Edward Vesala.
significance: An important entry in Tomasz Stańko's discography, showcasing their contribution to contemporary jazz.

title: Blue Notes for Mongezi
label: Ogun Recordings
description: Blue Notes for Mongezi is an album by The Blue Notes, featuring saxophonist Dudu Pukwana, pianist Chris McGregor, bassist Johnny Dyani, and drummer Louis Moholo. It was recorded on December 23, 1975, in a rehearsal room in London, and was released on vinyl as a double album in 1976 by Ogun Records. In 2022, Ogun reissued the album in expanded form as a double-CD set, using tracks that originally appeared on the 2008 compilation The Ogun Collection.
significance: An important entry in The Blue Notes's discography, showcasing their contribution to hard bop.
keyTracks: Blue Notes for Mongezi: First Movement
Blue Notes for Mongezi: Second Movement
Blue Notes for Mongezi: Third Movement
Blue Notes for Mongezi: Fourth Movement

title: Bright Size Life
label: ECM
description: Bright Size Life is the debut album by Pat Metheny, recorded in December 1975 and released on ECM in March of the following year. The recording features his working trio with Jaco Pastorius and Bob Moses.
significance: Launched Metheny's influential career and showcased a new direction for jazz guitar. His ECM sound influenced a generation.

title: Flowers for Albert
label: India Navigation
description: David Murray burst onto the New York loft jazz scene with this stunning debut, paying tribute to Albert Ayler. The young tenor saxophonist displayed astonishing maturity, channeling Ayler's raw spiritual power while adding his own bluesy warmth and structural sophistication.
significance: Announced Murray as the most important new voice in avant-garde jazz since the 1960s. His ability to synthesize the entire tenor saxophone tradition—from Ben Webster to Albert Ayler—set him apart from his peers.

title: Gateway
label: ECM
description: Gateway is the debut album by jazz trio Gateway, recorded in March 1975 and released on ECM the following year. The trio comprises guitarist John Abercrombie, bassist Dave Holland and drummer Jack DeJohnette.
significance: Defined a new approach to jazz trio—spacious, dynamic, and collectively improvised. The ECM production enhanced the music's intimacy.

title: Gnu High
label: ECM
description: Gnu High is an album by Canadian trumpeter and composer Kenny Wheeler recorded in June 1975 and released on ECM the following year. The quartet features rhythm section Keith Jarrett, Dave Holland and Jack DeJohnette.
significance: Established Wheeler as a major composer and bandleader. The album's beauty and sophistication influenced a generation of composers.

title: Guitar Solos 2
label: Caroline Records
description: Guitar Solos 2 is the second in a series of three albums of improvised guitar solos by various musicians. It was released in the United Kingdom by Caroline Records in 1976, and consists of two guitar solos by Fred Frith, three by Derek Bailey, three by Hans Reichel and one by G. F. Fitzgerald. Frith coordinated and produced the series, which began with his 1974 debut solo album, Guitar Solos.
significance: An important entry in Derek Bailey's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Jaco Pastorius
label: Epic
description: Jaco Pastorius is the debut solo album by Jaco Pastorius, released in 1976 by Epic Records. The album was produced by Bobby Colomby, drummer and founder of Blood, Sweat & Tears.
significance: Reinvented the electric bass as a lead and harmonic instrument. His technique—harmonics, distortion, melodic brilliance—influenced every bassist who followed.

title: Open Your Eyes You Can Fly
label: Milestone
description: Brazilian vocalist Flora Purim's finest solo album showcases her extraordinary four-octave range and wordless vocal improvisation over a bed of lush fusion arrangements. With Airto Moreira on percussion and a stellar cast of West Coast musicians, the music soars between samba, jazz, and cosmic fusion.
significance: Established Purim as the most important Brazilian jazz vocalist of her generation. Her ability to use her voice as an instrument—scatting, wailing, whispering—expanded the vocabulary of jazz vocals.
keyTracks: Open Your Eyes You Can Fly

title: Scenery
label: Trio Records
description: Self-taught Sapporo pianist Ryo Fukui's stunning debut, recorded when he was just 28. His crystalline touch and remarkable technique shine through standards like 'It Could Happen to You' and 'Early Summer,' blending Bill Evans' lyricism with McCoy Tyner's fire.
significance: Virtually unknown outside Japan for decades, the album became a viral sensation when uploaded to YouTube in the 2010s, introducing millions to Japanese jazz and Fukui's extraordinary talent.

title: Shakti with John McLaughlin
label: Columbia
description: Shakti with John McLaughlin is a 1976 world fusion album by Shakti, released on the Columbia label. The album blends elements of world fusion, Indian classical. Notable tracks include "Joy", "Lotus Feet", "What Need Have I for This", "India".
significance: The most successful fusion of jazz and Indian classical music. McLaughlin's devotion to Indian tradition earned respect from purists.
keyTracks: What Need Have I for This

title: Spaceball
label: Arista
description: Spaceball is a 1976 cool jazz album by Larry Young, released on the Arista label. Notable tracks include "Moonwalk", "Startripper", "Sticky Wicket", "Flytime".
significance: An important entry in Larry Young's discography, showcasing their contribution to cool jazz.

title: The People's Republic
label: Horizon
description: The Revolutionary Ensemble—violinist Leroy Jenkins, bassist Sirone, and drummer Jerome Cooper—was one of the most radical and innovative groups of the loft jazz era. Their music combines extended techniques, free improvisation, and an acute sensitivity to texture and dynamics.
significance: Represented the most radical possibilities of the loft jazz movement. Their instrumentation (violin, bass, drums) and egalitarian group concept broke every convention of jazz ensemble performance.

title: Waiting
label: Blue Note
description:
Waiting is an album by American jazz vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson recorded in 1976 and released on the Blue Note label. The sessions were released on CD as part of Mosaic Select: Bobby Hutcherson in 2007.
The group is tight and well composed throughout the 6 original pieces, allowing Bobby to reach a free, rapidly changing score.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Bobby Hutcherson's evolving artistic vision.
keyTracks: Don't Be Afraid (To Fall in Love Again)

title: We’ll Be Together Again
label: Unknown
description: We’ll Be Together Again is a 1976 jazz album by Pat Martino. Notable tracks include "Open Road (Olee/Variations and Song/Open Road)", "Lament", "We'll Be Together Again", "You Don't Know What Love Is".
significance: An important entry in Pat Martino's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: You Don't Know What Love Is

title: Youngblood
label: Pablo
description: Youngblood is a 1976 jazz album by Jon Faddis, released on the Pablo label. Notable tracks include "Here 'Tis", "Gershwin Prelude # 2", "Round Midnight", "Be Bop (Dizzy'z Fingers)".
significance: An important entry in Jon Faddis's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Azimuth
label: ECM
description: Azimuth is the debut album by British jazz trio Azimuth recorded in March 1977 and released on ECM later that year. The trio consists of pianist John Taylor, vocalist Norma Winstone, and trumpeter Kenny Wheeler.
significance: Defined British ECM jazz. The trio's delicate interplay influenced the European chamber jazz movement.

title: Dança das Cabeças
label: ECM
description: Gismonti's duet with percussionist Nana Vasconcelos is a spellbinding conversation between guitar, piano, and an array of Brazilian percussion. The music evokes the vast landscapes of Brazil—from Amazonian forests to arid sertão—with an intimacy that feels like eavesdropping on nature.
significance: One of ECM's most beloved albums and a landmark in Brazilian music. The Gismonti-Vasconcelos partnership created a new template for acoustic world music that transcended jazz, classical, and folk categories.

title: Diamond Express
label: Arista
description: Diamond Express is an album by South African alto saxophonist and composer Dudu Pukwana. It was recorded during the fall of 1975 at Island Studios in London, and was released on vinyl in 1977 by Arista/Freedom. In 1999, it was reissued on CD by the German Jazz Colours label with the title Ubagile, and with a different track order. On four of the album's five tracks, Pukwana is joined by trumpeter Mongezi Feza, guitarist Lucky Ranko, keyboardist Frank Roberts, double bassist Ernest Mothle, and drummers James Meine and Louis Moholo.
significance: An important entry in Dudu Pukwana's discography, showcasing their contribution to free jazz.
keyTracks: Tete and Barbs in My Mind

title: Dinner Music
label: Watt
description: Dinner Music is a 1977 jazz rock album by Carla Bley, released on the Watt label. The album blends elements of jazz rock, avant-garde jazz. Notable tracks include "Ida Lupino", "Wrong Key Donkey", "Dining Alone", "Sing Me Softly of the Blues".
significance: Demonstrated Bley's wit and her ability to work in accessible forms without sacrificing intelligence. The album's humor is distinctly European.
keyTracks: Sing Me Softly of the Blues

title: Easy Living
label: Unknown
description: Easy Living is an album by jazz saxophonist Sonny Rollins, released on the Milestone label in 1977, featuring performances by Rollins with George Duke, Paul Jackson and Tony Williams with Byron Miller and Bill Summers added on one track.
significance: A noteworthy album in Sonny Rollins's body of work within the jazz fusion tradition.

title: Emerald Tears
label: ECM
description: British bassist Dave Holland's solo recital is one of the most remarkable bass recordings in jazz. Moving between arco and pizzicato techniques, Holland draws an astonishing range of music from the acoustic bass—from singing melodies to percussive rhythms to lush double-stopped harmonies.
significance: Demonstrated that the acoustic bass could sustain a full-length solo album with variety and emotional depth. Holland's virtuosity and musical intelligence set a new standard for unaccompanied bass performance.

title: Heavy Weather
label: Columbia
description: Heavy Weather is the seventh album by Weather Report, released in 1977 through Columbia Records. By 1991, the release had sold 1,000,000 copies in the US alone; it would prove to be the band's most commercially successful album and one of the best sellers in the Columbia jazz catalog. DownBeat magazine gave Heavy Weather a five-star review, and later its readers voted it jazz album of the year.
significance: Proved fusion could be sophisticated and commercially successful. Pastorius's fretless bass playing changed the instrument forever.

title: Juice
label: RVC
description: Juice is a 1977 jazz-funk album by Ryo Kawasaki, released on the RVC label. The album blends elements of jazz-funk, jazz fusion. Notable tracks include "Juice", "Raisins", "Bamboo Child", "Little Tree".
significance: A cult classic of Japanese jazz-funk. Kawasaki's guitar synthesizer pioneering influenced later electronic jazz.

title: Mellow Dream
label: Trio Records
description: The follow-up to Scenery finds Fukui in an even more confident and exploratory mood. The trio swings hard through originals and standards, with Fukui's rapid-fire runs and delicate ballad work revealing the full range of his artistry.
significance: Confirmed Fukui's debut was no fluke. Along with Scenery, it documented one of Japan's most gifted pianists at his creative peak in Sapporo's intimate jazz scene.

title: Music Is My Sanctuary
label: Capitol Records
description: Music Is My Sanctuary is a 1977 jazz album by Gary Bartz, released on the Capitol Records label. The album blends elements of jazz, jazz fusion, jazz-funk. Notable tracks include "Music Is My Sanctuary", "Carnaval De L'esprit", "Love Ballad", "Swing Thing".
significance: An important entry in Gary Bartz's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Sit on It!
label: Mercury Records
description: A showcase for Jimmy Smith's organ, Sit on It! (1977) draws from the jazz vocabulary with authority. The album channels the electric energy and cross-genre experimentation of the fusion era. The set list features 'Give Up the Booty' and 'Can’t Hide Love' among its standout moments.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Jimmy Smith's evolving artistic vision.
keyTracks: Born to Groove / From You to Me to You

title: Slaves Mass
label: Warner Bros. Records
description: The eccentric Brazilian multi-instrumentalist's most adventurous album features him playing everything from flute to tuned pigs. Pascoal's extraordinary musicality transforms dense polyrhythmic textures and wild timbral experiments into music of startling beauty and humor.
significance: Revealed Pascoal as one of the most original musical minds of the twentieth century. His refusal to distinguish between 'natural' and 'musical' sounds anticipated entire movements in experimental music.

title: Tetterettet
label: ICP
description: The Instant Composers Pool Orchestra, led by Dutch drummer Han Bennink and pianist Misha Mengelberg, brings Dadaist humor and theatrical absurdity to free jazz. The music careens between composed passages, free improvisation, and outright chaos with gleeful abandon.
significance: Defined the Dutch approach to free improvisation, which embraced humor, theatricality, and Monk-influenced composition alongside total freedom. The ICP's playful spirit was a counterweight to the earnestness of much European free music.

title: Time Capsule
label: Vanguard
description: Time Capsule is a 1977 jazz album by Elvin Jones, released on the Vanguard label. Notable tracks include "Frost Bite", "Digital Display", "Moon Dance", "Time Capsule".
significance: An important entry in Elvin Jones's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: 1-OQA+19
label: Black Saint
description: 1-OQA+19 is a 1978 free jazz album by Muhal Richard Abrams, released on the Black Saint label. The album blends elements of free jazz, jazz. Notable tracks include "Charlie in the Parker", "Balladi", "Arhythm Song", "OQA".
significance: An important entry in Muhal Richard Abrams's discography, showcasing their contribution to free jazz.

title: African Violet
label: ABC Impulse!
description: African Violet is an album by American trumpeter Blue Mitchell which features arrangements by McKinley Mitchell recorded in 1977 and released on the Impulse! label in 1978.
significance: A noteworthy album in Blue Mitchell's body of work within the jazz fusion tradition.

title: Exotic Mysteries
label: Columbia
description: Exotic Mysteries is a 1978 jazz album by Lonnie Liston Smith, released on the Columbia label. The album blends elements of jazz, jazz-funk. Notable tracks include "Space Princess", "Quiet Moments", "Magical Journey", "Exotic Mysteries".
significance: An important entry in Lonnie Liston Smith's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Lanquidity
label: Philly Jazz
description: Lanquidity is a 1978 ambient jazz album by Sun Ra, released on the Philly Jazz label. The album blends elements of ambient jazz, space jazz, funk. Notable tracks include "Lanquidity", "Where Pathways Meet", "That's How I Feel", "Twin Stars of Thence".
significance: Proof that Sun Ra could create deeply hypnotic music without sacrificing his cosmic vision. The album has been rediscovered by electronic music producers seeking organic textures.

title: Little Red’s Fantasy
label: Muse Records
description: Little Red’s Fantasy documents a 1978 studio date for Muse Records, with Woody Shaw leading a tight jazz ensemble. The album channels the electric energy and cross-genre experimentation of the fusion era. Among its key performances are 'Jean Marie' and 'Sashianova'.
significance: A noteworthy album in Woody Shaw's body of work within the jazz fusion tradition.

title: My Song
label: ECM
description: My Song is an album by jazz musician Keith Jarrett, recorded in November 1977 and released on ECM in June the following year—the second release from his "European Quartet" featuring saxophonist Jan Garbarek and rhythm section, Palle Danielsson and Jon Christensen, after Belonging (1974).
significance: Defined the ECM aesthetic: spacious, lyrical, and Nordic. The European Quartet's sound influenced countless musicians.

title: Nature’s Revenge
label: MPS
description: Nature’s Revenge is a 1978 jazz album by Ryo Kawasaki, released on the MPS label. Notable tracks include "Nature’s Revenge", "Body and Soul", "Choro", "The Straw That Broke the Lion’s Back".
significance: An important entry in Ryo Kawasaki's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: The Straw That Broke the Lion’s Back

title: Rosewood
label: Columbia
description: Rosewood is a 1978 hard bop album by Woody Shaw, released on the Columbia label. The album blends elements of hard bop, jazz. Notable tracks include "Rosewood", "Every Time I See You", "The Legend of Cheops", "Rahsaan's Run".
significance: An important entry in Woody Shaw's discography, showcasing their contribution to hard bop.

title: School
label: Parachute
description: School is a 1978 jazz fusion album by John Zorn, released on the Parachute label. Notable tracks include "Solitude", "Duet", "The Return of Romance", "The Shreeve".
significance: An important entry in John Zorn's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz fusion.

title: Spirits Rejoice!
label: Ogun
description: The great South African drummer's debut as a leader, featuring an international ensemble of free jazz luminaries including Evan Parker and Keith Tippett. Moholo's drumming brings African polyrhythmic power to the European free improvisation tradition.
significance: A crucial document of the cross-pollination between South African and European free jazz. Moholo's rhythmic vitality and spiritual intensity gave the British free scene a new dimension.
keyTracks: You Ain't Gonna Know Me 'Cos You Think You Know Me

title: Streams of Consciousness
label: Baystate
description: Streams of Consciousness is a 1978 jazz album by Max Roach, released on the Baystate label. Notable tracks include "Streams of Consciousness", "Inception", "Acclamation", "Consanguinity".
significance: An important entry in Max Roach's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Summer Soft
label: ABC Records
description: Summer Soft is an album by American trumpeter Blue Mitchell, recorded in 1977 and released on the Impulse! label in 1978. It was his final album.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Blue Mitchell's evolving artistic vision.
keyTracks: Love Has Made Me a Dreamer

title: The Cecil Taylor Unit
label: New World Records
description: On The Cecil Taylor Unit (1978), Cecil Taylor delivers a focused exploration of free jazz, recorded for New World Records. The set list features 'Idut' and 'Serdab' among its standout moments.
significance: An important entry in Cecil Taylor's discography, showcasing their contribution to free jazz.

title: Third Plane
label: JVC
description: Third Plane is a 1978 jazz album by Tony Williams, released on the JVC label. The album blends elements of jazz, post-bop. Notable tracks include "Third Plane", "Quiet Times", "Lawra", "Stella by Starlight".
significance: An important entry in Tony Williams's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Third Plane
label: JVC
description: Third Plane is an album by jazz bassist Ron Carter, released on the Milestone label in 1977. It features performances by Carter, Herbie Hancock and Tony Williams.
significance: Demonstrates Herbie Hancock's command of jazz and their place within the broader jazz lineage.

title: You Send Me
label: Polydor
description: You Send Me is a studio album by American musician Roy Ayers. It was released in 1978 through Polydor Records. Recording sessions for the album took place at Sigma Sound Studios and Electric Lady Studios in New York City. Production was handled by Roy Ayers and Carla Vaughn.
significance: An important entry in Roy Ayers's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: Get on Up, Get on Down

title: Codona
label: ECM
description: Codona is an album by the American sitarist and tabla player Collin Walcott, American jazz trumpeter Don Cherry and Brazilian jazz percussionist Naná Vasconcelos. It was recorded in September 1978 and released on ECM the following year — the first of three self-titled albums by the trio.
significance: Pioneered the fusion of jazz with global folk traditions. The trio's approach influenced countless world music projects.

title: Gitane
label: All Life
description: Gitane is an album by American jazz bassist Charlie Haden and French gypsy jazz guitarist Christian Escoudé, released in 1979. Most of the album's compositions were written by guitarist Django Reinhardt.
significance: An important entry in Charlie Haden's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: In His Good Time
label: Ogun Recordings
description: In His Good Time is a live solo piano album by Chris McGregor. Dedicated to "the ONE who brings all things to be in His Good Time," it was recorded on November 18, 1977, at the Palais des Glaces in Paris, and was released on LP in 1979 by Ogun Records. In 2012, Ogun reissued the album on CD in expanded form, with the first eight tracks presenting the concert's first set as a continuous performance, and the remaining tracks extracted from the second set. The music includes originals, traditional pieces, and tunes composed by McGregor's colleagues Mongezi Feza and Dudu Pukwana.
significance: An important entry in Chris McGregor's discography, showcasing their contribution to free jazz.

title: In the Tradition
label: Columbia
description: Blythe's second Columbia album reinterprets jazz standards and originals with his signature blend of free jazz intensity and deep tradition. With Fred Hopkins on bass and Steve McCall on drums, Blythe tears through 'In a Sentimental Mood' and Fats Waller's 'Jitterbug Waltz' with fierce, loving energy.
significance: Demonstrated that the avant-garde and tradition need not be opposed. Blythe's ability to play 'inside' and 'outside' simultaneously made him one of the most important saxophonists of the late 1970s.

title: Lenox Avenue Breakdown
label: Columbia
description: Alto saxophonist Arthur Blythe's major label debut is a thrilling collision of free jazz energy, hard bop swing, and street-level funk. His soaring, vocalized tone—one of the most distinctive in jazz—wails over James Newton's flute and Bob Stewart's tuba with ecstatic abandon.
significance: Proved that avant-garde jazz could work on a major label without compromise. Blythe's combination of free jazz fire and deep blues feeling created a uniquely accessible brand of creative music.

title: Nice Guys
label: ECM
description: Nice Guys is an album by the Art Ensemble of Chicago, recorded in May 1978 and released on ECM the following year—their debut for the label. The quintet features trumpeter Lester Bowie, saxophonists Joseph Jarman and Roscoe Mitchell and rhythm section Malachi Favors Maghostut and Famoudou Don Moye.
significance: Introduced the Art Ensemble to wider audiences through ECM's distribution. The album's playful approach challenged stereotypes about avant-garde jazz being humorless.

title: Oasis
label: Horo Records
description: Oasis is a 1979 jazz album by Jeanne Lee, released on the Horo Records label. Notable tracks include "Oasis", "Wellspring, Op. 341", "Opus 169", "Communication".
significance: An important entry in Jeanne Lee's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: One Excerpt From "The Jamican Suit" By Jeanne Lee

title: Oblique
label: Blue Note
description: Oblique is an album by vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson, featuring performances by Herbie Hancock, Albert Stinson, and Joe Chambers. The album was recorded on July 21, 1967. Oblique was Hutcherson's second recording in a quartet setting, after Happenings, The personnel on Happenings are identical, save the replacement of Bob Cranshaw with Stinson,. Blue Note did not release the album until 1979, as a limited edition in Japan, followed by a regular issue in 1990.
significance: A noteworthy album in Bobby Hutcherson's body of work within the jazz fusion tradition.

title: Photo With Blue Sky, White Cloud, Wires, Windows and a Red Roof
label: ECM
description: Photo With Blue Sky, White Cloud, Wires, Windows and a Red Roof is a 1979 contemporary jazz album by Jan Garbarek, released on the ECM label. The album blends elements of contemporary jazz, Nordic jazz. Notable tracks include "Parce Mihi Domine", "Lillekort", "Entering".
significance: Helped define European jazz as distinct from American traditions. Garbarek's influence on Scandinavian jazz was profound.

title: Soapsuds, Soapsuds
label: Artists House
description: Soapsuds, Soapsuds is an album of duets by saxophonist/trumpeter Ornette Coleman and bassist Charlie Haden, recorded in 1977 and released on the Artists House label.
significance: An essential document of Ornette Coleman's artistry during the jazz fusion period.

title: Tales of Captain Black
label: DIW
description: James Blood Ulmer's debut as a leader applies Ornette Coleman's harmolodic theory to raw, funky electric guitar with ferocious results. With Coleman himself on alto saxophone and Denardo Coleman on drums, the album deconstructs blues and funk through the lens of free jazz.
significance: Launched the harmolodic guitar movement and proved that Coleman's theoretical innovations could generate visceral, body-moving music. Ulmer's jagged, distorted guitar work influenced punk, no wave, and alternative rock.

title: The Audience with Betty Carter
label: Bet-Car
description: The Audience with Betty Carter is a 1979 vocal jazz album by Betty Carter, released on the Bet-Car label. The album blends elements of vocal jazz, bebop. Notable tracks include "Sounds (Movin' On)", "I Think I Got It Now", "My Favorite Things", "The Trolley Song".
significance: The definitive document of Carter's genius. Her ability to deconstruct and reconstruct melodies in real time is unmatched in jazz vocals.
keyTracks: I Think I Got It Now

title: The Joy of Flying
label: CBS
description:
The Joy of Flying is a jazz fusion album by Tony Williams. It was recorded at the end of the Tony Williams Lifetime years, and is considered his first solo album since 1966. It included three duets, two with Mahavishnu Orchestra keyboardist Jan Hammer, and one with free jazz pianist Cecil Taylor, and three different quartets: the first featured Hammer along with guitarist George Benson and bassist
Paul Jackson, the second featured pianist Herbie Hancock, bassist Stanley Clarke and Tom Scott on Lyricon, and the third featured guitarist Ronnie Montrose, keyboardist Brian Auger, and bass guitarist Mario Cipollina. "Hip Skip" also featured a five-piece horn section.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Tony Williams's evolving artistic vision.

title: Very R.A.R.E.
label: Trio
description: Very R.A.R.E. is an album by drummer Elvin Jones, recorded in 1979 and released on the Japanese Trio label.
significance: Demonstrates Elvin Jones's command of bebop and their place within the broader jazz lineage.

title: African Marketplace
label: Elektra Musician
description: African Marketplace is a 1980 Cape jazz album by Abdullah Ibrahim, released on the Elektra Musician label. The album blends elements of Cape jazz, world jazz. Notable tracks include "African Marketplace", "Zimbabwe", "The Wedding", "Soweto".
significance: Introduced Ibrahim to wider American audiences. The album's positive energy contrasted with the despair of apartheid.

title: Dance With Death
label: Blue Note
description: A 1980 Blue Note session, Dance With Death finds Andrew Hill pushing deeper into jazz territory. The album reflects the stylistic breadth and global awareness of contemporary jazz. Tracks such as 'Yellow Violet' and 'Partitions' anchor the program.
significance: An important entry in Andrew Hill's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Idylle und Katastrophen
label: Po Torch Records
description: Idylle und Katastrophen is a 1980 free jazz album by Alexander von Schlippenbach, released on the Po Torch Records label. The album blends elements of free jazz, jazz. Notable tracks include "Der Schornsteinfeger: Der Schornsteinfeger / Im Taubenschlag / Entfernungen", "Paul Solo", "Bonbonbäcker Asturiet", "Ensemble I".
significance: An important entry in Alexander von Schlippenbach's discography, showcasing their contribution to free jazz.
keyTracks: Der Schornsteinfeger: Der Schornsteinfeger / Im Taubenschlag / Entfernungen

title: Inner Glow
label: Blue Note
description: Inner Glow is an album by American jazz vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson recorded in 1975 and originally released on the Japanese Blue Note label. While never issued in the U.S. the tracks were included on the Mosaic Records box set Mosaic Select 26: Bobby Hutcherson, released in 2007.
significance: An essential document of Bobby Hutcherson's artistry during the modern jazz period.

title: Medina
label: Blue Note Classic
description: Medina is an album by jazz vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson. It was released in 1980 on Blue Note Records featuring six tracks recorded on 11 August 1969. The CD reissue added five tracks (#7–11) recorded in 1968, previously released on vinyl in 1979, under the title Spiral.
significance: Demonstrates Bobby Hutcherson's command of jazz and their place within the broader jazz lineage.

title: Revue
label: Black Saint
description: The World Saxophone Quartet—Julius Hemphill, Oliver Lake, David Murray, and Hamiet Bluiett—created a new paradigm: four saxophones with no rhythm section, blending composed arrangements with collective free improvisation. Revue showcases their full range, from Ellington-esque swing to volcanic free blowing.
significance: Established the saxophone quartet as a viable jazz ensemble and proved that four horns could generate all the rhythm, harmony, and melody a band needs. The WSQ's concept spawned dozens of similar groups worldwide.

title: Silver ’n Strings Play the Music of the Spheres
label: Blue Note
description: On Silver ’n Strings Play the Music of the Spheres (1980), Horace Silver delivers a focused exploration of contemporary jazz, recorded for Blue Note. Tracks such as 'The Search for Direction / Direction Discovered' and 'We All Have a Part to Play' anchor the program.
significance: Demonstrates Horace Silver's command of contemporary jazz and their place within the broader jazz lineage.
keyTracks: The Search for Direction / Direction Discovered
We All Have a Part to Play

title: Spihumonesty
label: Black Saint
description: Spihumonesty is an album by Muhal Richard Abrams, released on the Italian Black Saint label in 1979. It features performances by Abrams, George Lewis, Roscoe Mitchell, Amina Claudine Myers, Youseff Yancy, Leonard Jones, and Jay Clayton.
significance: An important entry in Muhal Richard Abrams's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: The Believer
label: Baystate
description: The Believer is a 1980 contemporary jazz album by Billy Harper, released on the Baystate label. The album blends elements of contemporary jazz, jazz. Notable tracks include "Is It Not True, Simpy Because You Cannot Believe It ?", "I Do Believe", "Believe, For It Is True".
significance: An important entry in Billy Harper's discography, showcasing their contribution to contemporary jazz.
keyTracks: Is It Not True, Simpy Because You Cannot Believe It ?

title: Herbie Hancock Trio with Ron Carter + Tony Williams
label: CBS/Sony
description: On Herbie Hancock Trio with Ron Carter + Tony Williams (1981), Tony Williams delivers a focused exploration of jazz, recorded for CBS/Sony. The album reflects the stylistic breadth and global awareness of contemporary jazz. Among its key performances are 'Stablemates' and 'Dolphin Dance'.
significance: A noteworthy album in Tony Williams's body of work within the modern jazz tradition.

title: Mistral
label: Liberty
description: Mistral is a studio album by jazz trumpeter Freddie Hubbard that was released by Eastworld and Liberty with Phil Ranelin, Art Pepper, George Cables, Peter Wolf, Roland Bautista, Stanley Clarke, Peter Erskine, and Paulinho da Costa.
significance: An important entry in Freddie Hubbard's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Saudades
label: ECM
description: Saudades is a 1981 Brazilian jazz album by Egberto Gismonti, released on the ECM label. The album blends elements of Brazilian jazz, chamber jazz. Notable tracks include "Palhaço", "Dança das Cabeças", "Frevo", "Baião Malandro".
significance: One of the finest cross-cultural collaborations in jazz. Gismonti's guitar and piano work spans classical, jazz, and Brazilian folk.

title: Social Studies
label: Watt
description: Social Studies is an album by American composer, bandleader and keyboardist Carla Bley, recorded in 1980 and released on the Watt/ECM label in 1981.
significance: Showed that avant-garde sensibilities could work in a big band format. Bley's arrangements are witty, sophisticated, and swinging.

title: The Creeper
label: Blue Note Classic
description: The Creeper is an album by American trumpeter Donald Byrd. Along with Byrd, the album Sonny Red, Pepper Adams, Chick Corea, Miroslav Vitous, and Mickey Roker. It was recorded in October 1967 but not released on the Blue Note label until 1981.
significance: An important entry in Donald Byrd's discography, showcasing their contribution to hard bop.

title: The Source
label: Jazz Man
description: The Source is a live album by trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie recorded in Paris, France, in 1973 and first released on the French America label.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Dizzy Gillespie's evolving artistic vision.

title: Turi
label: Areito
description: Turi is a 1981 afro-cuban jazz album by Arturo Sandoval, released on the Areito label. The album blends elements of afro-cuban jazz, jazz, latin jazz. Notable tracks include "Luna de miel en Guama", "Claudia", "Blue en dos partes", "María".
significance: An important entry in Arturo Sandoval's discography, showcasing their contribution to afro-cuban jazz.

title: Word of Mouth
label: Warner Bros. Records
description: Word of Mouth is the second and final solo studio album by American bassist Jaco Pastorius, released in 1981, while he was still a member of the jazz fusion group Weather Report, and also the name of a big band that Pastorius assembled and with which he toured in 1982.
