significance: Showed Pastorius's compositional ambitions beyond bass virtuosity. The big band arrangements were innovative but the album's excess hinted at troubles to come.

title: You Must Believe in Spring
label: Warner Bros. Records
description: You Must Believe in Spring is an album by American jazz pianist Bill Evans, recorded with bassist Eddie Gómez and drummer Eliot Zigmund in August 1977 and released in February 1981, shortly after Evans' death in September 1980.
significance: Demonstrates Bill Evans's command of jazz and their place within the broader jazz lineage.

title: 246:3AM
label: EXPRESS
description: 246:3AM is a 1982 jazz fusion album by Jiro Inagaki, released on the EXPRESS label. Notable tracks include "ジンで朝まで", "バハマ・エアポート", "海鳴りに誘われて", "蒼い追憶".
significance: An important entry in Jiro Inagaki's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz fusion.

title: Earth Jones
label: Quicksilver
description: Earth Jones is a 1982 bebop album by Elvin Jones, released on the Quicksilver label. Notable tracks include "Three Card Molly", "Is Seeing Believing?", "The Top of the Middle", "Earth Jones".
significance: An important entry in Elvin Jones's discography, showcasing their contribution to bebop.

title: Paths, Prints
label: ECM
description: Jan Garbarek's masterful quartet album with Bill Frisell on guitar, Eberhard Weber on bass, and Jon Christensen on drums distills the Norwegian saxophonist's vision into its purest form. The music breathes with the vast, austere beauty of Scandinavian landscapes, yet swings with surprising warmth.
significance: Represents the pinnacle of the ECM aesthetic and Garbarek's most perfectly realized album. The combination of Garbarek's icy tenor with Frisell's shimmering guitar created one of jazz's most distinctive and influential ensemble sounds.

title: Reel Life
label: Unknown
description: Reel Life is an album by jazz saxophonist Sonny Rollins, released on the Milestone label in 1982, featuring performances by Rollins with Bobby Broom, Yoshiaki Masuo, Bob Cranshaw and Jack DeJohnette.
significance: An important entry in Sonny Rollins's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Swingin' With Bud
label: Unknown
description: A showcase for Bud Powell's piano, Swingin' With Bud (1982) draws from the bebop vocabulary with authority. The album reflects the stylistic breadth and global awareness of contemporary jazz. Among its key performances are 'Another Dozen' and 'Like Someone in Love'.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Bud Powell's evolving artistic vision.

title: When Was That?
label: About Time
description: Threadgill's expanded ensemble, the Sextett (which actually contained seven members), showcases his increasingly complex compositional vision. The music is densely layered yet full of swing, with unusual instrumentation including two drummers and cello adding unique textures.
significance: Demonstrated Threadgill's evolution from free jazz improviser to one of America's most original composers. His genre-defying approach would eventually earn him the Pulitzer Prize for Music.

title: i.o.u.
label: [no label]
description: i.o.u. is a 1982 jazz fusion album by Allan Holdsworth, released on the [no label] label. Notable tracks include "The Things You See", "Where Is One", "Checking Out", "Letters of Marque".
significance: An important entry in Allan Holdsworth's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz fusion.

title: Future Shock
label: Columbia
description: Future Shock is the twenty-ninth album by American jazz pianist Herbie Hancock, released in August 1983 by Columbia Records. It was his first release from his electro-funk era and an early example of instrumental hip hop. Participating musicians include bass guitarist Bill Laswell, guitarist Pete Cosey and drummer Sly Dunbar.
significance: A noteworthy album in Herbie Hancock's body of work within the modern jazz tradition.

title: New York Improvisations
label: Elektra Musician
description: New York Improvisations is a 1983 free jazz album by Lennie Tristano, released on the Elektra Musician label. The album blends elements of free jazz, jazz. Notable tracks include "Manhattan Studio", "My Melacholy Baby", "Lover Man (Oh Where Can You Be)", "I'll See You in My Dreams".
significance: An important entry in Lennie Tristano's discography, showcasing their contribution to free jazz.
keyTracks: Lover Man (Oh Where Can You Be)
I'll See You in My Dreams
There Will Never Be Another You

title: Standards, Vol. 1
label: ECM
description: Standards, Vol. 1 is an album by American jazz pianist Keith Jarrett recorded over two days in January 1983 and released on ECM on cassette and LP later that year—a session which also produced Changes (1984) and Standards, Vol. 2 (1985). The trio features rhythm section Gary Peacock and Jack DeJohnette, the first release by the long-standing "Standards Trio".
significance: Launched one of jazz's most celebrated groups. Their approach to standards influenced generations of pianists.

title: The Meeting
label: Philips
description: On The Meeting (1983), Chick Corea delivers a focused exploration of free improvisation, recorded for Philips. Notable tracks include 'Someday My Prince Will Come' and 'Put Your Little Foot Out'.
significance: Adds to Chick Corea's reputation as a leading voice in free improvisation.

title: Baroque Music for Trumpets
label: CBS Records Masterworks
description: Baroque Music for Trumpets is a 1984 contemporary jazz album by Wynton Marsalis, released on the CBS Records Masterworks label. Notable tracks include "Concerto in D major for Trumpet, Oboes & Strings: I. Allegro", "Concerto in D major for Trumpet, Oboes & Strings: II. Largo", "Concerto in D major for Trumpet, Oboes & Strings: III. Allegro (Moderato)", ""Let the Bright Seraphim" from Samson".
significance: An important entry in Wynton Marsalis's discography, showcasing their contribution to contemporary jazz.
keyTracks: Concerto in D major for Trumpet, Oboes & Strings: I. Allegro
Concerto in D major for Trumpet, Oboes & Strings: II. Largo
Concerto in D major for Trumpet, Oboes & Strings: III. Allegro (Moderato)
Sonata A 5 for Trumpet and Strings, T.V.3

title: Domino Theory
label: CBS/Sony
description: Domino Theory is the twelfth studio album by jazz fusion band Weather Report, released in April 1984. It is the second studio album to feature the Hakim-Bailey-Rossy rhythm section. The album contains seven tracks, three of which were recorded live.
significance: An important entry in Weather Report's discography, showcasing their contribution to contemporary jazz.

title: Full Circle
label: Concord Jazz
description: Full Circle is a 1984 hard bop album by Bobby Hutcherson, released on the Concord Jazz label. The album blends elements of hard bop, jazz, post-bop. Notable tracks include "Marshall Arts", "Helen's Song", "On the Sunny Side of the Street", "Skylark".
significance: An important entry in Bobby Hutcherson's discography, showcasing their contribution to hard bop.
keyTracks: On the Sunny Side of the Street

title: Palo pá rumba
label: Unknown
description: Palo pá rumba is a 1984 jazz fusion album by Eddie Palmieri. Notable tracks include "Palo pá rumba", "1983", "Bomba de corazón", "Bajo con tumbao".
significance: An important entry in Eddie Palmieri's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz fusion.

title: Straight Life - The Savoy Sessions
label: Savoy Jazz
description: Straight Life - The Savoy Sessions, released in 1984, captures Art Pepper at a creative peak, blending jazz with personal expression. The album reflects the stylistic breadth and global awareness of contemporary jazz. Tracks such as 'Straight Life' and 'Chili Pepper' anchor the program.
significance: Adds to Art Pepper's reputation as a leading voice in jazz.

title: The Printmakers
label: Minor Music
description: The Printmakers is the first album by the pianist Geri Allen. It was recorded in February 1984 in Germany and released on the German Minor Music label.
significance: Introduced Allen as the leading pianist of her generation. Her synthesis of Cecil Taylor's energy and harmonic sophistication created a personal style.

title: Afro-Cuban Jazz
label: Unknown
description: Afro-Cuban jazz is the earliest form of Latin jazz. It mixes Afro-Cuban clave-based rhythms with jazz harmonies and techniques of improvisation. Afro-Cuban music has deep roots in African ritual and rhythm. The genre emerged in the early 1940s with the Cuban musicians Mario Bauzá and Frank Grillo "Machito" in the band Machito and his Afro-Cubans in New York City. In 1947, the collaborations of bebop trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie and percussionist Chano Pozo brought Afro-Cuban rhythms and instruments, such as the tumbadora and the bongo, into the East Coast jazz scene.
significance: An important entry in Mario Bauzá's discography, showcasing their contribution to swing.

title: Black Codes (From the Underground)
label: Columbia
description: Black Codes (From the Underground) is a 1985 post-bop album by Wynton Marsalis, released on the Columbia label. The album blends elements of post-bop, neo-traditional jazz. Notable tracks include "Black Codes", "For Wee Folks", "Delfeayo's Dilemma", "Phryzzinian Man".
significance: Announced the neo-traditional movement and proved that young musicians could engage with tradition without being trapped by it.

title: Cielo e terra
label: Direct Metal Mastering
description: Cielo e terra is a 1985 jazz fusion album by Al Di Meola, released on the Direct Metal Mastering label. The album blends elements of jazz fusion, jazz. Notable tracks include "Traces of a Tear", "Vertigo Shadow", "Cielo e terra", "Enigma of Desire".
significance: An important entry in Al Di Meola's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz fusion.

title: Hearts and Numbers
label: Hip Pocket Records
description: Hearts and Numbers is a 1985 contemporary jazz album by Michael Brecker, released on the Hip Pocket Records label. The album blends elements of contemporary jazz, jazz. Notable tracks include "Pointing at the Moon", "More Pointing", "Pools", "Regrets".
significance: An important entry in Michael Brecker's discography, showcasing their contribution to contemporary jazz.

title: Setting Standards
label: Muse Records
description: Setting Standards is an album led by trumpeter Woody Shaw which was recorded in 1983 and released on the Muse label. Setting Standards was released as part of Woody Shaw: The Complete Muse Sessions by Mosaic Records in 2013.
significance: An important entry in Woody Shaw's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Sportin’ Life
label: Columbia
description: Sportin’ Life, released in 1985, captures Weather Report at a creative peak, blending jazz with personal expression. The album reflects the stylistic breadth and global awareness of contemporary jazz. Tracks such as 'Corner Pocket' and 'Indiscretions' anchor the program.
significance: An important entry in Weather Report's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Stranger Than Love
label: Po Torch Records
description: Stranger Than Love is a 1985 free jazz album by Alexander von Schlippenbach, released on the Po Torch Records label. The album blends elements of free jazz, jazz. Notable tracks include "Stranger Than Love (1st Part)", "Stranger Than Love (Continued)".
significance: An important entry in Alexander von Schlippenbach's discography, showcasing their contribution to free jazz.

title: The Third Decade
label: ECM
description: The Third Decade is a 1985 avant-garde jazz album by Art Ensemble of Chicago, released on the ECM label. The album blends elements of avant-garde jazz, contemporary jazz, free jazz. Notable tracks include "Prayer for Jimbo Kwesi", "Funky AECO", "Walking in the Moonlight", "The Bell Piece".
significance: An important entry in Art Ensemble of Chicago's discography, showcasing their contribution to avant-garde jazz.

title: Tribal Tech
label: Passport
description: Tribal Tech is a 1985 jazz fusion album by Tribal Tech, released on the Passport label. Notable tracks include "Canine", "Stella", "Spears".
significance: Influenced a generation of fusion guitarists with its technical demands and inventive compositions.

title: When Spaceships Appear
label: El Saturn Records
description: On When Spaceships Appear (1985), Sun Ra delivers a focused exploration of avant-garde jazz, recorded for El Saturn Records. Highlights include 'When Spaceships Appear' and 'Fragile Emotions Blues'.
significance: An essential document of Sun Ra's artistry during the modern jazz period.

title: A música livre de Hermeto Paschoal
label: Fontana
description: A música livre de Hermeto Paschoal is a 1986 jazz album by Hermeto Pascoal, released on the Fontana label. The album blends elements of jazz, latin jazz.
significance: An important entry in Hermeto Pascoal's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Alma
label: Unknown
description: Alma is a 1986 contemporary jazz album by Egberto Gismonti. Notable tracks include "Baião malandro", "Palhaço", "Loro", "Maracatú".
significance: An important entry in Egberto Gismonti's discography, showcasing their contribution to contemporary jazz.

title: Benny Carter All Stars
label: Sonet
description: Benny Carter All Stars is a 1986 early jazz album by Red Norvo, released on the Sonet label. Notable tracks include "Easy Money", "Memories of You", "Here's That Rainy Day", "Blues for Lucky Lovers".
significance: An important entry in Red Norvo's discography, showcasing their contribution to early jazz.

title: Drum Night At Birdland
label: Vogue France
description: Drum Night At Birdland documents a 1986 studio date for Vogue France, with Art Blakey leading a tight hard bop ensemble. Among its key performances are 'Wee Dot' and 'Solo Philly Joe Jones'.
significance: A noteworthy album in Art Blakey's body of work within the modern jazz tradition.

title: Get Together
label: Pablo
description: Get Together is a 1986 jazz album by Count Basie, released on the Pablo label. The album blends elements of jazz, post-bop, swing. Notable tracks include "Ode to Pres", "Basie's Bag", "Swinging on the Cusp", "Like It Used to Be".
significance: A 1986 jazz release by Count Basie, representing their creative work during this period.

title: Legacy
label: Concord Jazz
description: Legacy is a 1986 jazz fusion album by Jon Faddis, released on the Concord Jazz label. Notable tracks include "West End Blues", "Little Jazz", "A Night In Tunisia", "Instigator".
significance: An important entry in Jon Faddis's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz fusion.

title: Music by Ellington & Bartók
label: EMI Digital
description: First released in 1986, Music by Ellington & Bartók is an essential chapter in Duke Ellington's recorded output, rooted in jazz. The album reflects the stylistic breadth and global awareness of contemporary jazz. Tracks such as 'Sonata for Solo Violin (1944): I. Tempo di ciaccona' and 'Sonata for Solo Violin (1944): II. Fuga - Risoluto, non troppo vivo' anchor the program.
significance: Demonstrates Duke Ellington's command of jazz and their place within the broader jazz lineage.
keyTracks: Sonata for Solo Violin (1944): I. Tempo di ciaccona
Sonata for Solo Violin (1944): II. Fuga - Risoluto, non troppo vivo
Sonata for Solo Violin (1944): III. Melodia - Adagio
Sonata for Solo Violin (1944): IV. Presto
Mainly Black (from Black, Brown and Beige Suite, arr. Nigel Kenney): I. Introduction (feat. double bass: Alec Dankworth)

title: Royal Garden Blues
label: CBS
description: Royal Garden Blues is a 1986 jazz album by Branford Marsalis, released on the CBS label. Notable tracks include "Swingin’ at the Haven", "Dienda", "Strike Up the Band", "Emanon".
significance: An important entry in Branford Marsalis's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: 'Round About Midnight at the Cafe Bohemia, Volume I
label: Blue Note
description: First released in 1987, 'Round About Midnight at the Cafe Bohemia, Volume I is an essential chapter in Kenny Dorham's recorded output, rooted in jazz. The album reflects the stylistic breadth and global awareness of contemporary jazz. The set list features 'Monaco' and ''Round About Midnight' among its standout moments.
significance: A noteworthy album in Kenny Dorham's body of work within the modern jazz tradition.

title: Civilization
label: Blue Note
description: Civilization is an album by the American musician Tony Williams, released in 1987. He supported it with a North American tour. The album peaked in the top five on Billboard's Jazz Albums chart.
significance: An important entry in Tony Williams's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Crystal Stair
label: Columbia
description: Crystal Stair is a 1987 jazz album by Donald Harrison, released on the Columbia label. The album blends elements of jazz, post-bop. Notable tracks include "Softly as in a Morning Sunrise", "Crystal Stair", "God Bless the Child", "Endicott".
significance: An important entry in Donald Harrison's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: Softly as in a Morning Sunrise

title: Crystal Stair
label: Columbia
description: Crystal Stair is a 1987 jazz album by Terence Blanchard, released on the Columbia label. The album blends elements of jazz, post-bop. Notable tracks include "Softly as in a Morning Sunrise", "Crystal Stair", "God Bless the Child", "Endicott".
significance: An important entry in Terence Blanchard's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: Softly as in a Morning Sunrise

title: Dances and Ballads
label: Elektra Nonesuch
description: Dances and Ballads is an album by the jazz group the World Saxophone Quartet, released in 1988 and featuring performances by Hamiet Bluiett, Julius Hemphill, Oliver Lake and David Murray.
significance: An important entry in World Saxophone Quartet's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Deliverance
label: Disques Espérance
description: Deliverance is a 1987 jazz fusion album by Manu Dibango, released on the Disques Espérance label. Notable tracks include "Tropical Garden", "Ah Freak Sans Fric", "Oh Oh", "Canta".
significance: An important entry in Manu Dibango's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz fusion.

title: Nightwind
label: Optimism, Incorporated
description: A showcase for Herbie Hancock's piano, Nightwind (1987) draws from the jazz vocabulary with authority. Notable tracks include 'When the Lights Go Out' and 'Nightwind'.
significance: An essential document of Herbie Hancock's artistry during the modern jazz period.

title: Quintessence
label: Enja Records
description: Quintessence is a 1987 jazz album by Terri Lyne Carrington, released on the Enja Records label. The album blends elements of jazz, latin jazz, spiritual jazz. Notable tracks include "For Now and Forever", "Lyons", "Univized", "Vamp for Ochun".
significance: An important entry in Terri Lyne Carrington's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Radebe: They Shoot to Kill
label: Affinity
description: Radebe: They Shoot to Kill is a 1987 jazz album by Dudu Pukwana, released on the Affinity label. Notable tracks include "Mbizo Radebe (They Shoot to Kill), Pt. 1", "Mbizo Radebe (They Shoot to Kill), Pt. 2".
significance: An important entry in Dudu Pukwana's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: Mbizo Radebe (They Shoot to Kill), Pt. 1
Mbizo Radebe (They Shoot to Kill), Pt. 2

title: Satchmo
label: Pickwick Compact Discs
description: Louis Daniel Armstrong, nicknamed "Satchmo", "Satch", and "Pops", was an American jazz and blues trumpeter and vocalist. He was among the most influential figures in jazz. His career spanned five decades and several eras in the history of jazz. Armstrong received numerous accolades including the Grammy Award for Best Male Vocal Performance for Hello, Dolly! in 1965, as well as a posthumous win for the Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award in 1972.
significance: An important entry in Louis Armstrong's discography, showcasing their contribution to early jazz.
keyTracks: I Want a Big Butter and Egg Man
(What Did I Have to do to Get) Black and Blue

title: The Healers
label: Black Saint
description: The Healers is a 1987 jazz album by Randy Weston, released on the Black Saint label. Notable tracks include "Clever Beggar", "The Healers", "Mbizo", "Blue Moses".
significance: An important entry in Randy Weston's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: The Inexhaustible Document
label: Matchless Recordings
description: The Inexhaustible Document is a 1987 jazz album by AMM, released on the Matchless Recordings label. Notable tracks include "Page 9:1", "Page 9:2".
significance: An important entry in AMM's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: The Racer
label: ProJazz
description: The Racer is a 1987 jazz album by Ronnie Foster, released on the ProJazz label. The album blends elements of jazz, jazz-funk. Notable tracks include "Europe", "The Racer", "Linne's Theme", "Squirt".
significance: An important entry in Ronnie Foster's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: Verona Rag
label: Soul Note
description: Verona Rag is an album by American jazz pianist Andrew Hill recorded in 1986 and released on the Italian Soul Note label in 1987. The solo album features three of Hill's original compositions and two jazz standards.
significance: Demonstrates Andrew Hill's command of jazz and their place within the broader jazz lineage.

title: Verses
label: Break Time
description: Verses is a 1987 hard bop album by Wallace Roney, released on the Break Time label. The album blends elements of hard bop, jazz, post-bop. Notable tracks include "Float", "Verses", "Blue in Green", "Topaz".
significance: An important entry in Wallace Roney's discography, showcasing their contribution to hard bop.

title: Blue Skies
label: JMT
description: Blue Skies is the third studio album by American jazz singer Cassandra Wilson. It was released on the JMT label in 1988 and features Wilson performing ten jazz standards accompanied by Mulgrew Miller on piano, Terri Lyne Carrington on drums, and Lonnie Plaxico on bass.
significance: An important entry in Cassandra Wilson's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: I've Grown Accustomed to His Face
I Didn't Know What Time It Was
Gee Baby Ain't I Good to You

title: Duke Ellington
label: Phontastic
description: Edward Kennedy "Duke" Ellington was an American jazz pianist, composer, and leader of his eponymous jazz orchestra from 1924 through the rest of his life. Ralph J. Gleason called him "America's most important composer."
significance: A noteworthy album in Duke Ellington's body of work within the modern jazz tradition.

title: Grandmothers Teaching
label: ITM Records
description: Grandmothers Teaching is a 1988 contemporary jazz album by Chris McGregor, released on the ITM Records label. The album blends elements of contemporary jazz, free jazz, jazz. Notable tracks include "Have You Noticed Spring Is a Good Time", "Put It Right", "Maxine", "I Will Let the Spring to Explain".
significance: An important entry in Chris McGregor's discography, showcasing their contribution to contemporary jazz.
keyTracks: Have You Noticed Spring Is a Good Time
I Will Let the Spring to Explain

title: Jazz at Storyville
label: Black Lion
description: With Jazz at Storyville (1988), Sidney Bechet crafts a jazz session that balances technical mastery and emotional depth. Tracks such as 'C Jam Blues' and 'Crazy Rhythm' anchor the program.
significance: Demonstrates Sidney Bechet's command of jazz and their place within the broader jazz lineage.

title: Look What I Got!
label: Verve
description: Look What I Got! is a 1988 vocal jazz album by Betty Carter, released on the Verve label. The album blends elements of vocal jazz, bebop. Notable tracks include "Look What I Got", "The Man I Love", "That Sunday, That Summer", "Tight".
significance: Introduced Carter to a new generation after years of self-produced independence. Her uncompromising artistry finally received mainstream recognition.

title: Oop-Pop-A-Da
label: Duke Street Records
description: Oop-Pop-A-Da is an album by the Moe Koffman Quintet featuring trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie recorded in 1988 and released on the Soundwing label.
significance: Adds to Dizzy Gillespie's reputation as a leading voice in jazz.

title: Ritual: The Modern Jazz Messengers
label: Pacific Jazz
description: Ritual is a studio album by the Jazz Messengers featuring Art Blakey. Three years after being recorded, it was first released on the Pacific Jazz Records label as PJM-402, and reissued by them in 1962, with an Elmo Hope session, as Art Blakey & The Jazz Messengers/The Elmo Hope Quintet* Featuring Harold Land as PJ-33. It has been reissued on CD.
significance: Adds to Art Blakey's reputation as a leading voice in jazz.

title: Shades of Change
label: Enja Records
description: Shades of Change is a 1988 contemporary jazz album by Geri Allen, released on the Enja Records label. The album blends elements of contemporary jazz, jazz. Notable tracks include "Shades of a Labyrinth", "Out of a Labyrinth", "3 + 1 = 5", "The Search".
significance: An important entry in Geri Allen's discography, showcasing their contribution to contemporary jazz.

title: Stories
label: Unknown
description: Stories is a 1988 free jazz album by Tom Harrell. The album blends elements of free jazz, avant-garde jazz. Notable tracks include "Rapture", "Songflower", "The Mountain", "The Water’s Edge".
significance: An important entry in Tom Harrell's discography, showcasing their contribution to free jazz.

title: The Eternal Triangle
label: Blue Note
description: The Eternal Triangle is an album by trumpeters Freddie Hubbard and Woody Shaw recorded in June 1987 and released on the Blue Note label. It features performances by Hubbard, Shaw, Ray Drummond, Carl Allen, Mulgrew Miller and Kenny Garrett. The album followed the first Hubbard/Shaw collaboration Double Take (1985) and the two volumes were combined for the double CD release The Complete Freddie Hubbard and Woody Shaw Sessions (1995).
significance: An important entry in Woody Shaw's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

title: The Gil Evans Orchestra Plays the Music of Jimi Hendrix
label: BMG
description: The Gil Evans Orchestra Plays the Music of Jimi Hendrix is an album of Jimi Hendrix's compositions by jazz composer, conductor and pianist Gil Evans. The music was arranged by Evans and members of his orchestra. The album was recorded in 1974 and performed by Evans with an orchestra featuring David Sanborn, Howard Johnson, Billy Harper, and John Abercrombie. The album was re-released with additional tracks on CD in 1988.
significance: An important entry in Gil Evans's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: Castles Made of Sand / Foxey Lady
Up From the Skies (take 1 - original master)
1983 - A Merman I Should Turn to Be

title: Close Enough for Love
label: Verve
description: Close Enough for Love is a 1989 studio album by Shirley Horn. It was her second album; her first studio album, was on the Verve Records label.
significance: Demonstrated that less could be more in jazz vocals. Horn's influence on Diana Krall and other singer-pianists was profound.

title: Cool Cat
label: Timeless Records

significance: An important entry in Chet Baker's discography, showcasing their contribution to cool jazz.

title: Fat Jazz
label: Fresh Sound Records
description: Fat Jazz, also referred to as Jackie McLean Plays Fat Jazz, is an album by American saxophonist Jackie McLean, recorded in late 1957 and released by the Jubilee label in 1959. It features McLean in a sextet with trumpeter Webster Young, tuba player Ray Draper, pianist Gil Coggins, bassist George Tucker and drummer Larry Ritchie.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Jackie McLean's evolving artistic vision.
keyTracks: What Good Am I Without You?

title: Incoming
label: K‐tel
description: Incoming is a 1989 afro-cuban jazz album by Billy Cobham, released on the K‐tel label. The album blends elements of afro-cuban jazz, avant-garde jazz, cool jazz. Notable tracks include "Incoming", "Cuba on the Horizon", "Caribbean Curry", "One From the Heart".
significance: A 1989 afro-cuban jazz release by Billy Cobham, representing their creative work during this period.

title: Jazz Street
label: Timeless Records
description: Red Rose Speedway is the second studio album by the British-American rock band Wings, although credited to "Paul McCartney and Wings". It was released through Apple Records in the United States on 30 April 1973 and in the United Kingdom on 4 May, preceded by its lead single, the ballad "My Love". The artist name change was made in the belief that the public's unfamiliarity with the band had been responsible for the weak commercial performance of the group's 1971 debut album Wild Life.
significance: Demonstrates Jaco Pastorius's command of jazz and their place within the broader jazz lineage.

title: Live Sessions at Minton's Playhouse

significance: An important entry in Charlie Christian's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

significance: An important entry in Michel Camilo's discography, showcasing their contribution to latin jazz.

title: Piano Reflections
label: Capitol Jazz
description: The Duke Plays Ellington is an album by American pianist, composer and bandleader Duke Ellington featuring trio sessions recorded for the Capitol label in 1953. The album was rereleased with additional tracks on CD as Piano Reflections in 1989
significance: A noteworthy album in Duke Ellington's body of work within the modern jazz tradition.

title: Pittsburgh
label: Atlantic
description: Pittsburgh is an album by the American jazz pianist Ahmad Jamal. It was recorded in 1989 and released on the Atlantic label. It is a tribute to Jamal's hometown. The album was recorded in Chicago.
significance: A noteworthy album in Ahmad Jamal's body of work within the modern jazz tradition.

title: Solo
label: CBS
description: Issued by CBS in 1989, Solo presents Thelonious Monk in a bebop context that highlights their improvisational gifts. The album reflects the stylistic breadth and global awareness of contemporary jazz. The set list features 'Dinah' and 'I Surrender, Dear' among its standout moments.
significance: Adds to Thelonious Monk's reputation as a leading voice in bebop.

title: The Best Thing for You
label: A&M Records
description: The Best Thing for You is an album by trumpeter Chet Baker which was recorded in 1977 but not released on the A&M label until 1989, after the performer's death. The tracks were rereleased as part of the double CD reissue of You Can't Go Home Again in 2000 with previously unreleased tracks and alternate takes.
significance: Adds to Chet Baker's reputation as a leading voice in jazz.
keyTracks: I’m Getting Sentimental Over You / You’ve Changed
If You Could See Me Now

title: Twylight
label: Minor Music
description: Twylight is a 1989 post-bop album by Geri Allen, released on the Minor Music label. The album blends elements of post-bop, avant-garde jazz. Notable tracks include "Twylight", "Lonely Woman", "For John Malachi", "In Appreciation".
significance: Demonstrated Allen's ability to work with jazz legends on equal terms. The intergenerational dialogue produced some of her finest work.

title: West 42nd Street
label: Black Lion
description: A showcase for Kenny Dorham's trumpet, West 42nd Street (1989) draws from the hard bop vocabulary with authority. Among its key performances are 'Avars (take 3)' and 'Stella by Starlight (take 1)'.
significance: An essential document of Kenny Dorham's artistry during the modern jazz period.

title: Question and Answer
label: Geffen Records

Question and Answer is an album by guitarist Pat Metheny with acoustic bassist Dave Holland and drummer Roy Haynes.
Metheny won the 1991 Grammy Award for Best Instrumental Composition for "Change of Heart".

title: Satchmo
label: Sony Music
description: Louis Daniel Armstrong, nicknamed "Satchmo", "Satch", and "Pops", was an American jazz and blues trumpeter and vocalist. He was among the most influential figures in jazz. His career spanned five decades and several eras in the history of jazz. Armstrong received numerous accolades including the Grammy Award for Best Male Vocal Performance for Hello, Dolly! in 1965, as well as a posthumous win for the Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award in 1972.
significance: Demonstrates Louis Armstrong's command of early jazz and their place within the broader jazz lineage.

title: Ask the Ages
label: Axiom
description: Sonny Sharrock's magnum opus, produced by Bill Laswell, features Pharoah Sanders, Elvin Jones, and Charnett Moffett in a supergroup that channels the fire of late-period Coltrane through Sharrock's savage, distortion-drenched guitar. The music is simultaneously spiritual and explosive.
significance: The greatest jazz guitar album that almost nobody heard on release. Sharrock died just three years later, making this his testament—a blazing proof that electric guitar could achieve the same spiritual intensity as Coltrane's saxophone.
keyTracks: Who Does She Hope to Be?

title: In Private
label: Fresh Sound Records
description: "In Private" is a song by British singer Dusty Springfield, released as a single on 20 November 1989 by Parlophone and EMI. It was Springfield's third single in a row to be a chart success, after an absence of nearly two decades from the charts. Both "In Private" and Springfield's previous single, "Nothing Has Been Proved" were produced by Pet Shop Boys, who helped return Springfield to prominence with their 1987 hit collaboration "What Have I Done to Deserve This?". Both Springfield singles were included on her 1990 British album Reputation.
significance: An important entry in Art Tatum's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

significance: Represents a key chapter in Count Basie's evolving artistic vision.

title: Celebration
label: World Circuit
description: South African pianist and multi-instrumentalist Bheki Mseleku's triumphant debut, featuring Pharoah Sanders, Joe Henderson, and Elvin Jones. The album radiates spiritual warmth, combining Coltrane-influenced modal jazz with the melodic richness of South African church music.
significance: Introduced Mseleku to the international jazz world and demonstrated the continued vitality of the South African jazz tradition. His ability to unite American and African jazz masters was remarkable.

title: Double Trouble
label: Unknown

The album balances accessible melodies with improvisational depth, reflecting Ayers's ability to bridge jazz, funk, and R&B.
significance: An important entry in Roy Ayers's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz fusion.

title: I Heard You Twice the First Time
label: Columbia
description: I Heard You Twice the First Time is a jazz album by Branford Marsalis that explores different aspects of the blues, featuring guest appearances from B.B. King, John Lee Hooker, Russell Malone, Wynton Marsalis and Linda Hopkins. It peaked at number 1 on the Top Jazz Albums chart. The album won the Grammy Award for Best Jazz Instrumental Performance, Individual or Group.
significance: An important entry in Branford Marsalis's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: Brother Trying to Catch a Cab (on the Eastside) Blues

title: In the Key of Oscar
label: Music of the loons
description: Oscar Emmanuel Peterson was a Canadian jazz pianist and composer. As a virtuoso who is considered to be one of the greatest jazz pianists of all time, Peterson released more than 200 recordings, won eight Grammy Awards, as well as a lifetime achievement award from the Recording Academy, and received numerous other awards and honours. He played thousands of concerts worldwide in a career lasting more than 60 years. He was called the "Maharaja of the keyboard" by Duke Ellington, simply "O.P." by his friends, and was informally known in the jazz community as "the King of inside swing".
significance: An important entry in Oscar Peterson's discography, showcasing their contribution to contemporary jazz.
keyTracks: Medley: Sweet Lady/Better Luck Next Time/Waltz for Debby

title: Blue Light 'Til Dawn
label: Blue Note
description: Blue Light 'til Dawn is a studio album by American jazz singer Cassandra Wilson. Her first album on the Blue Note label, it was released in 1993. It contains Wilson's interpretations of songs by various blues and rock artists, as well as three original compositions. The album marked a shift in Wilson’s recording style, mostly dropping the electric instruments of her earlier albums in favor of acoustic arrangements. A critical and commercial breakthrough, the album was re-released in 2014 with three bonus tracks recorded live somewhere in Europe during the Blue Light 'til Dawn Tour.
significance: Revitalized jazz vocals for a new generation. Wilson's approach—rooted but experimental—influenced countless singers.

title: Just You, Just Me
label: Telarc Jazz
description: Just You, Just Me is a 1994 album by Dave Brubeck. This album is the 3rd of Brubeck's solo works preceded by Brubeck Plays Brubeck and Plays and Plays and.... There was a 37-year gap between "Brubeck Plays and Plays" and this album with Brubeck focusing on working with his quartet during that time. Brubeck writes that for this album, "...I prefer to record in the same way as I play at home..." with all of the songs on this album being first takes with no advanced editing. The exception to this is "I Understand" of which Brubeck did three takes playing each in different ways.
significance: An important entry in Dave Brubeck's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.
keyTracks: It's the Talk of the Town
Variations on Brother, Can You Spare a Dime?

Since the late 1970s, he has recorded and toured with Frank Zappa, Joni Mitchell, and Sting, among many other appearances in the studio and in concert.

title: Soul of Things
label: ECM
description: Polish trumpeter Tomasz Stańko's haunting album with pianist Marcin Wasilewski's young trio is a masterclass in mood and space. Stańko's dark, fragile tone—shaped by decades of experience from free jazz to Krzysztof Komeda's film scores—hovers over the trio's luminous accompaniment.
significance: Introduced Stańko and the Wasilewski trio to international audiences and became one of ECM's best-selling albums of the 2000s. The music's Eastern European melancholy and beauty created a new standard for contemporary European jazz.

title: Swings
label: High Definition Jazz
description: A showcase for Art Tatum's piano, Swings (2002) draws from the jazz vocabulary with authority. The album reflects the stylistic breadth and global awareness of contemporary jazz. Highlights include 'I Know That You Know' and 'Fifty Second Street Blues'.
significance: An essential document of Art Tatum's artistry during the modern jazz period.

title: Heartcore
label: Verve Music Group
description: Heartcore is Kurt Rosenwinkel's fifth album as a band leader. The album was fully produced by Rosenwinkel and Q-Tip of popular hip-hop group A Tribe Called Quest. The album is a departure musically from Rosenwinkel's previous work, as he contributes keyboard, drums, and voice, at times creating soundscapes completely on his own in his personal studio. Says Rosenwinkel, "There is a place, musically, that’s above the categories. This record – it’s jazz. And it’s much more".

description: Land of Giants is an album by McCoy Tyner released on the Telarc label in 2003. It was recorded in December 2002 and features performances of Tyner with vibraphonist Bobby Hutcherson, bassist Charnett Moffett and drummer Eric Harland. The Allmusic review by Matt Collar states that "While the work here is by no means as provocative as the stuff Hutcherson and Tyner produced in their heydays, it nonetheless proves them to be utter masters of the straight-ahead modern jazz idiom and should appeal to longtime fans".
significance: Represents a key chapter in McCoy Tyner's evolving artistic vision.

title: Concert in the Garden
label: ArtistShare
description: Maria Schneider's Grammy-winning album was the first internet-only release to win a Grammy Award. Her orchestra creates music of luminous beauty, drawing on Brazilian rhythms, Gil Evans-inspired orchestration, and nature imagery to produce some of the most gorgeous large-ensemble jazz of the 21st century.
significance: Transformed the music industry by proving that fan-funded, internet-distributed music could achieve the highest artistic recognition. Schneider's compositions represent the pinnacle of modern jazz orchestration.

title: Pimp Master

significance: Proved that jazz could compete with rock and electronic music for sheer visceral excitement. Their club-oriented approach brought young Japanese audiences back to jazz in unprecedented numbers.

description: Inner Cry Blues is an album by trombonist and composer Grachan Moncur III. It was recorded in February 2007, and was released by Lunar Module Records later that year. On the album, Moncur is accompanied by saxophonist Mitch Marcus, trumpeter Erik Jekabson, vibraphonist Ben Adams, bassist Lukas Vesely, and drummer Sameer Gupta. Issued three years after Exploration, it was Moncur's second album following a lengthy hiatus, during which he wrote, taught, and dealt with dental issues.

description: Ruff Draft is an EP reissued as a studio album by American hip hop producer and rapper J Dilla released under the moniker "Jay Dee". It was originally released in February 2003 as an EP, by his then-newly founded label, Mummy Records, and distributed by Groove Attack, a German record label. In 2007, Ruff Draft was extended, remastered, and posthumously re-released as a "solo album" by Stones Throw Records. The re-release sold 8,049 copies in its first week, J Dilla's largest first-week sales as a solo artist.

title: Infernal Machines
label: New Amsterdam
description: Darcy James Argue's Secret Society delivers a big band album for the 21st century, drawing on influences from Thad Jones to Radiohead. The 18-piece ensemble navigates complex, ever-shifting compositions with power and precision, creating a steampunk-inflected noir atmosphere.
significance: Reinvented the jazz big band for a new generation. Argue's ability to synthesize indie rock, electronica, and jazz traditions into compelling large-ensemble music made him a leading voice in contemporary orchestral jazz.

title: Pithecanthropus Erectus + The Clown
label: Essential Jazz Classics
description: Pithecanthropus Erectus + The Clown, released in 2009, captures Charles Mingus at a creative peak, blending jazz with personal expression. The album reflects the stylistic breadth and global awareness of contemporary jazz. Notable tracks include 'Pithecanthropus Erectus' and 'A Foggy Day'.
significance: A noteworthy album in Charles Mingus's body of work within the modern jazz tradition.

title: Time Waits
label: Blue Note
description: Time Waits, also known as The Amazing Bud Powell, Vol. 4, is a studio album by American jazz pianist Bud Powell recorded at Van Gelder Studio on May 24, 1958 with rhythm section Sam Jones and Philly Joe Jones and released on Blue Note later that year.
significance: Represents a key chapter in Bud Powell's evolving artistic vision.

title: Ballads
label: Music and Melody
description: Ballads is a jazz album by John Coltrane released in January 1963 by Impulse! Records. It was recorded in December 1961 and 1962, and released with catalogue number A-32 (mono) and AS-32 (stereo). Critic Gene Lees stated that the quartet had never played the tunes before. "They arrived with music-store sheet music of the songs" and just before the recordings, they "would discuss each tune, write out copies of the changes they'd use, semi-rehearse for a half hour and then do it". Each piece was recorded in one take, except for "All or Nothing at All".
significance: Represents a key chapter in John Coltrane's evolving artistic vision.

title: Jasmine
label: ECM
description:
Jasmine is a 2010 duet album by American pianist Keith Jarrett and bassist Charlie Haden. It was released on 12 May 2010.
The album was recorded in March 2007 at Jarrett's home studio in Oxford Township, New Jersey during the recording of a documentary about Haden. Additional tracks from the same session, including alternate takes of "Where Can I Go Without You" and "Goodbye", were released in 2014 on Last Dance.
significance: An important entry in Charlie Haden's discography, showcasing their contribution to jazz.

Intro / I’m Gonna Laugh You Right Out of My Life

title: Coin Coin Chapter Two: Mississippi Moonchile
label: Constellation
description: The second installment in alto saxophonist Matana Roberts' epic Coin Coin series excavates her family's Mississippi roots through a kaleidoscopic mix of free jazz, field recordings, spoken word, and spectral folk melodies. The album is at once deeply personal and universally resonant.
significance: Established Roberts as one of the most important conceptual artists in contemporary music. Her 'panoramic sound quilting' approach created a new form for addressing the African-American experience through improvised music.
keyTracks: How Much Would You Pay for a Shadow

title: Sleeping Beauty
label: Strut
description: Sleeping Beauty is an album by jazz composer, bandleader and keyboardist Sun Ra and his Intergalactic Myth Science Solar Arkestra, recorded in 1979 and originally released on Ra's Saturn label and rereleased on CD on Art Yard in 2008.
significance: Demonstrates Sun Ra's command of swing and their place within the broader jazz lineage.
